Latin/digits OK inside these entity values even for Indic documents: AADHAAR_NUMBER, PAN_NUMBER, PASSPORT_NUMBER, DRIVING_LICENCE, VOTER_ID, BPL_RATION_CARD, TAX_ID, PHONE_NUMBER, TELEPHONE_LANDLINE, EMAIL_ADDRESS, PIN_CODE, IP_ADDRESS, URL, BANK_ACCOUNT_NUMBER, IFSC_CODE, BANK_ROUTING_NUMBER, CREDIT_CARD_NUMBER, CVV, PIN, EMPLOYEE_ID, STUDENT_ID, IMEI_NUMBER, MAC_ADDRESS, VEHICLE_REGISTRATION, ABHA_ID, ABHA_ADDRESS, MRN, PATIENT_ID, HOSPITAL_ID, ADMISSION_NUMBER, ENCOUNTER_ID, APPOINTMENT_ID, REFERRAL_ID, INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER, BED_NUMBER, WARD_NUMBER
Do NOT invent types like DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER. Put calendar dates/times in plain prose.

Quality bar (BAD vs GOOD — avoid known judge/auditor failures):
Aim for PERFECT documents. Avoid these known failure modes:

BAD → dialect_script_impurity:
  English (or wrong-script) clinical body when Gujarati/Tamil/Meitei/Ol Chiki/Arabic/… is required.
GOOD:
  Full clinical prose in the assigned language AND exact script. Latin digits only inside ID tags
  (Aadhaar, phone, MRN, IFSC, email). Drug/lab names may stay Latin.

BAD → instruction_drift:
  Assigned domain is oncology but the note narrates generic TB / unrelated complaint.
GOOD:
  Chief complaint, findings, and plan clearly match the assigned clinical domain.

BAD → surrogate_plausibility_collapse:
  Persona district=Faridkot but VILLAGE/HOSPITAL in another state; ABHA local-part unrelated to PATIENT_NAME.
GOOD:
  All places grounded in persona state+district; names and ABHA coherent with PATIENT_NAME.

BAD → domain_persona_mismatch:
  Male persona with maternal delivery; age 70 with paediatric content;
  geriatric / extreme-age routine 28-week pregnancy.
GOOD:
  Sex/age in narrative and tags match persona exactly; maternal content is
  age-plausible (no unsafe extreme late pregnancy as a routine story).

BAD → missing tags / HOSPITAL_ID only:
  Facility name in prose, only [[HOSPITAL_ID|…]] tagged; doctor name untagged.
GOOD:
  [[HOSPITAL_NAME|…]] AND [[HOSPITAL_ID|…]]; [[DOCTOR_NAME|…]] whenever a doctor is mentioned.

BAD → entity stuffing / long SMS:
  AGE/PHONE/EMAIL repeated every chat turn; SMS that is a full discharge dump.
GOOD:
  Each TYPE once (twice max); SMS ≤ ~400 chars with every mandatory tag once.

BAD → invented types / bad checksums:
  [[DATE|…]], [[POLICY_NUMBER|…]]; Aadhaar failing Verhoeff; toy phones like 1234567890.
GOOD:
  Only allow-listed TYPEs (billing: EMAIL→EMAIL_ADDRESS, ADDRESS→RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS;
  receipt/state/money stay plain prose). Valid-looking synthetic Aadhaar/phone.

BAD → localized / translated TYPE names (kills tag protection):
  [[রোগীর_নাম|Anjali]], [[रोगी_नाम|…]], [[PatientName|…]], [[एम.आर.एन|…]].
GOOD:
  Exact ASCII allow-list only: [[PATIENT_NAME|Anjali Mondal]], [[MRN|MRN-…]].
  Translate prose later — NEVER translate the TYPE token.

BAD → repair oscillation (fix A by breaking B):
  Round1 invents DATE to fit a table → Round2 English rewrite to clear invent →
  Round3 dialect_script_impurity → Round4 invent again.
GOOD:
  Fix ALL known issues together: correct ASCII tags + correct domain/persona +
  correct target script (or English pivot if this is the pre-translate stage).
  Never English-rewrite a finished Indic body just to repair tags.

Language SLA:
  hi/ta/te/bn/mr/… must stay fluent with tags — failures are our prompt/repair bug.
  brx/mni/sat (rare scripts) may soft-fail; do not collapse the whole run for them.
payload.user_prompt:
Write one complete OPD Slip (Outpatient Department) for clinical domain Tuberculosis & Non-Communicable Diseases.

GENERATION LANGUAGE: English (Latin script) — this is an English pivot draft. A later pipeline stage will translate clinical prose into Punjabi (code=pa, script=Gurmukhi). Keep [[TYPE|value]] tags intact and valid.
CRITICAL: TYPE names are NEVER translated or localized — always exact ASCII allow-list (PATIENT_NAME, MRN, …). Forbidden: [[রোগীর_নাম|…]], [[रोगी_नाम|…]], [[PatientName|…]].
ID-like tagged values stay Latin/digits. Drug names / labs stay untagged.
DOMAIN ANCHOR: chief complaint, findings, and plan MUST match Tuberculosis & Non-Communicable Diseases — do not default to unrelated TB/generic content unless that is the assigned domain.
GEO ANCHOR: any DISTRICT/VILLAGE/HOSPITAL_NAME must be plausible for persona state=Punjab, district=Moga.
NOTE: translation stage (not this draft) will put the body into Gurmukhi for Punjabi.

PLACEMENT (OPD header): First line MUST include BOTH [[HOSPITAL_NAME|…]] (facility name) AND [[HOSPITAL_ID|…]] (facility code) — never substitute one for the other.
Put [[EMPLOYEE_ID|…]] on the registrar/clerk line and [[RELATIVE_NAME|…]] on the attendant/relative line. Include [[DOB|YYYY-MM-DD]] and [[OCCUPATION|…]] with demographics.

Persona anchors (must remain consistent; patient = this persona):
- sex / gender: Male
- age: 18
- marital_status: Never Married
- occupation: Stall and Market Salespersons, Other
- education_level: Matric/Secondary
- zone: Urban
- state: Punjab
- district: Moga
- first_language: Punjabi
- clinical_persona_summary: Salkhan Varma is an 18-year-old male from Moga, Punjab, who works as a market salesperson with a matriculation education. He is never married, lives in an urban setting, and speaks Punjabi as his first language with functional Hindi. His lifestyle includes late-night comic reading and listening to Bhangra music.

Document format guide for OPD Slip (Outpatient Department) (English structure; a later stage translates to the target language):
Example skeleton (adapt language/script; keep tags):
OPD Slip | [[HOSPITAL_NAME|District Hospital X]] | ID [[HOSPITAL_ID|DH-X-01]]
Patient: [[PATIENT_NAME|...]] | DOB [[DOB|1992-03-14]] | Age: [[AGE|34]] | Gender: [[GENDER|Female]]
Occupation: [[OCCUPATION|Teacher]] | MRN: [[MRN|OPD-2024-001]] | Doctor: [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. ...]]
Relative: [[RELATIVE_NAME|...]] | Phone: [[PHONE_NUMBER|98XXXXXXXX]]
Registrar EmpID: [[EMPLOYEE_ID|EMP-4412]] | District: [[DISTRICT|...]]
Chief complaint: <short clinical line matching assigned domain>

MANDATORY inline entities (13 types) — EACH must appear ≥ once as [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]. Missing ANY is a hard failure:
- PATIENT_NAME — Patient Name e.g. [[PATIENT_NAME|Meena Devi]]
- AGE — Age e.g. [[AGE|34]]
- GENDER — Gender e.g. [[GENDER|Female]]
- DOB — Date of Birth e.g. [[DOB|1992-03-14]]
- OCCUPATION — Occupation e.g. [[OCCUPATION|Teacher]]
- DOCTOR_NAME — Doctor Name e.g. [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Anjali Sharma]]
- HOSPITAL_NAME — Hospital / Institution Name e.g. [[HOSPITAL_NAME|District Hospital Kanpur]]
- HOSPITAL_ID — Hospital ID e.g. [[HOSPITAL_ID|HSP-UP-014]]
- DISTRICT — District e.g. [[DISTRICT|Kanpur Nagar]]
- MRN — Medical Record Number (MRN) e.g. [[MRN|MRN-2024-0815-001]]
- PHONE_NUMBER — Phone Number e.g. [[PHONE_NUMBER|9876512340]]
- RELATIVE_NAME — Relative Name (Father/Mother/Spouse/Guardian) e.g. [[RELATIVE_NAME|Ramesh Devi]]
- EMPLOYEE_ID — Employee ID e.g. [[EMPLOYEE_ID|EMP-77821]]

Before finishing, mentally checklist every mandatory TYPE above.
Do NOT invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, TOTAL_AMOUNT, GST_AMOUNT, GRAND_TOTAL). Put dates/times and money amounts in plain prose.
Produce ONLY the clinical document text with inline annotations. No markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.

ਮਰੀਜ਼: [[PATIENT_NAME|Salkhan Varma]] | ਜਨਮ ਮਿਤੀ [[DOB|2006-05-15]] | ਉਮਰ: [[AGE|18]] | ਲਿੰਗ: [[GENDER|Male]]
ਕਿੱਤਾ: [[OCCUPATION|Stall and Market Salespersons, Other]] | MRN: [[MRN|OPD-2024-MOG-001]] | ਡਾਕਟਰ: [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Gurpreet Singh]]
ਸੰਬੰਧੀ: [[RELATIVE_NAME|Balwinder Singh]] | ਫ਼ੋਨ: [[PHONE_NUMBER|9876543210]]
ਰਜਿਸਟਰਾਰ EmpID: [[EMPLOYEE_ID|REG-2024-015]] | ਜ਼ਿਲ੍ਹਾ: [[DISTRICT|Moga]]

ਮੁੱਖ ਸ਼ਿਕਾਇਤ: 3 ਹਫ਼ਤਿਆਂ ਤੋਂ ਲਗਾਤਾਰ ਖੰਘ, ਸ਼ਾਮ ਨੂੰ ਬੁਖਾਰ ਅਤੇ ਭਾਰ ਘਟਣਾ

ਮੌਜੂਦਾ ਬਿਮਾਰੀ ਦਾ ਇਤਿਹਾਸ:
ਮਰੀਜ਼ 3 ਹਫ਼ਤਿਆਂ ਤੋਂ ਉਤਪਾਦਕ ਖੰਘ ਨਾਲ ਪੇਸ਼ ਹੈ। ਸ਼ੁਰੂ ਵਿੱਚ ਖੁਸ਼ਕ ਸੀ, ਫਿਰ ਘੱਟ ਬਲਗ਼ਮ ਨਾਲ ਉਤਪਾਦਕ ਹੋ ਗਈ।
2 ਹਫ਼ਤਿਆਂ ਤੋਂ ਘੱਟ ਬੁਖਾਰ, ਸ਼ਾਮ ਨੂੰ ਬੁਖਾਰ ਅਤੇ ਲਗਭਗ 3 ਕਿਲੋ ਭਾਰ ਘਟਣਾ।
ਖੂਨ ਦੀ ਉਲਟੀ (ਹੀਮੋਪਟਾਈਸਿਸ) ਦੀ ਕੋਈ ਰਿਪੋਰਟ ਨਹੀਂ ਹੈ।

ਪੁਰਾਣੀ ਬਿਮਾਰੀ ਦਾ ਇਤਿਹਾਸ:
ਮਰੀਜ਼ ਮਿਹਨਤ ਕਰਨ 'ਤੇ ਕਦੇ-ਕਦੇ ਸਾਹ ਚੜ੍ਹਨ ਦੀ ਰਿਪੋਰਟ ਕਰਦਾ ਹੈ।
ਸ਼ੂਗਰ ਜਾਂ ਹਾਈ ਬਲੱਡ ਪ੍ਰੈਸ਼ਰ ਦੀ ਕੋਈ ਜਾਣੀ-ਪਛਾਣੀ ਬਿਮਾਰੀ ਨਹੀਂ ਹੈ।
ਪਰਿਵਾਰਕ ਇਤਿਹਾਸ: ਪਿਤਾ ਨੂੰ ਸ਼ੂਗਰ (ਡਾਇਬੀਟੀਜ਼ ਮੈਲੀਟਸ) ਹੈ।

ਜਾਂਚ ਦੇ ਨਤੀਜੇ:
- ਆਮ: ਪਤਲੀ ਬਣਤਰ, ਕਮਜ਼ੋਰ ਦਿੱਖ, BMI 17.2 kg/m²
- ਸਾਹ ਪ੍ਰਣਾਲੀ: ਤੇਜ਼ ਸਾਹ (RR 22/min), ਸੱਜੇ ਉੱਪਰਲੇ ਹਿੱਸੇ ਵਿੱਚ ਦੋਵੇਂ ਪਾਸੇ ਦੀਆਂ ਆਵਾਜ਼ਾਂ
- ਦਿਲ: ਦਿਲ ਦੀਆਂ ਆਵਾਜ਼ਾਂ ਸਾਧਾਰਨ, ਕੋਈ ਮਰਮਰ ਨਹੀਂ
- ਹੋਰ: ਉਂਗਲਾਂ ਦਾ ਕਲੱਬਿੰਗ ਨਹੀਂ, ਕੋਈ ਸਾਇਨੋਸਿਸ (ਨੀਲਾਪਣ) ਨਹੀਂ

ਹੁਕਮ ਦਿੱਤੀਆਂ ਗਈਆਂ ਜਾਂਚਾਂ:
- ਛਾਤੀ ਦਾ ਐਕਸ-ਰੇ: ਸੱਜੇ ਉੱਪਰਲੇ ਹਿੱਸੇ ਵਿੱਚ ਇਨਫਿਲਟਰੇਟ ਕੈਵਿਟੇਸ਼ਨ ਦੇ ਨਾਲ
- AFB ਲਈ ਬਲਗ਼ਮ: ਪਾਜ਼ੇਟਿਵ
- ਖੂਨ ਦੀ ਸ਼ੂਗਰ: ਖਾਲੀ ਪੇਟ 110 mg/dL
- HbA1c: 6.8%

ਨਿਦਾਨ:
1. ਫੇਫੜਿਆਂ ਦੀ ਤਪਦਿਕ (ਨਵੀਂ ਬਿਮਾਰੀ)
2. ਪ੍ਰੀ-ਡਾਇਬੀਟੀਜ਼

ਇਲਾਜ ਯੋਜਨਾ:
- ਤਪਦਿਕ ਵਿਰੋਧੀ ਥੈਰੇਪੀ: DOTS ਰੈਜੀਮੈਨ (Isoniazid, Rifampicin, Pyrazinamide, Ethambutol)
- ਪੋਸ਼ਣ ਸਹਾਇਤਾ ਅਤੇ ਵਿਟਾਮਿਨ ਸਪਲੀਮੈਂਟ
- ਪ੍ਰੀ-ਡਾਇਬੀਟੀਜ਼ ਲਈ ਜੀਵਨ ਸ਼ੈਲੀ ਵਿੱਚ ਬਦਲਾਅ
- 2 ਹਫ਼ਤਿਆਂ ਬਾਅਦ DOTS ਸੈਂਟਰ ਵਿੱਚ ਫਾਲੋ-ਅੱਪ

ਸਲਾਹ: ਇਲਾਜ ਦਾ ਪੂਰਾ ਕੋਰਸ ਪੂਰਾ ਕਰੋ, ਚੰਗਾ ਪੋਸ਼ਣ ਬਣਾਈ ਰੱਖੋ, ਸ਼ਰਾਬ ਅਤੇ ਤੰਬਾਕੂ ਤੋਂ ਪਰਹੇਜ਼ ਕਰੋ।
payload.generated_text_en:
OPD Slip | [[HOSPITAL_NAME|District Hospital Moga]] | ID [[HOSPITAL_ID|DH-MOG-001]]
Patient: [[PATIENT_NAME|Salkhan Varma]] | DOB [[DOB|2006-05-15]] | Age: [[AGE|18]] | Gender: [[GENDER|Male]]
Occupation: [[OCCUPATION|Stall and Market Salespersons, Other]] | MRN: [[MRN|OPD-2024-MOG-001]] | Doctor: [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Gurpreet Singh]]
Relative: [[RELATIVE_NAME|Balwinder Singh]] | Phone: [[PHONE_NUMBER|9876543210]]
Registrar EmpID: [[EMPLOYEE_ID|REG-2024-015]] | District: [[DISTRICT|Moga]]

Chief complaint: Persistent cough for 3 weeks with evening fever and weight loss

History of present illness: Patient presents with productive cough for 3 weeks, initially dry then became productive with scanty sputum. Associated with low-grade evening fever for 2 weeks and progressive weight loss of approximately 3 kg. No hemoptysis reported.

Past medical history: Patient reports occasional episodes of breathlessness on exertion. No known diabetes or hypertension. Family history positive for diabetes mellitus in father.

Examination findings:
- General: Thin build, cachectic appearance, BMI 17.2 kg/m²
- Respiratory: Tachypneic (RR 22/min), bilateral crackles in right upper zone
- Cardiovascular: Regular heart sounds, no murmurs
- Other: No clubbing, no cyanosis

Investigations ordered:
- Chest X-ray: Right upper lobe infiltrate with cavitation
- Sputum for AFB: Positive
- Blood sugar: Fasting 110 mg/dL
- HbA1c: 6.8%

Diagnosis:
1. Pulmonary Tuberculosis (New case)
2. Pre-diabetes

Treatment plan:
- Anti-tubercular therapy: DOTS regimen (Isoniazid, Rifampicin, Pyrazinamide, Ethambutol)
- Nutritional support and vitamin supplements
- Lifestyle modification for pre-diabetes
- Follow-up in DOTS center after 2 weeks

Advice: Complete full course of treatment, maintain good nutrition, avoid alcohol and tobacco.

payload.professional_persona: Neelam Bala, a 29‑year‑old aspiring home‑based tiffin caterer, combines rigorous household budgeting, precise meal planning and rudimentary spreadsheet skills to deliver punctual, high‑quality regional meals, and they competitively build a support network of fellow homemakers.
payload.linguistic_persona: Neelam Bala speaks Punjabi at home using Gurmukhi, reads and writes Hindi in Devanagari for community dealings, and possesses just enough English to recognize product labels, though they remain limited to simple sentences.
payload.sports_persona: Neelam Bala maintains fitness through daily morning walks and yoga, follows the Punjab Kings in the IPL and enjoys watching local kabaddi tournaments, while they occasionally join a community cricket viewing club with friends.
payload.arts_persona: Neelam Bala relaxes by stitching Phulkari embroidery, reads Punjabi short stories by Amrita Pritam, and listens to folk icons like Gurdas Maan and Harbhajan Mann, while they occasionally hum bhajans during chores.
payload.travel_persona: Neelam Bala prefers well‑planned short trips to nearby hill stations like Shimla and Manali or cultural visits to Amritsar’s Golden Temple, balancing adventure with budget constraints and they often travel with a close circle of family friends.
payload.culinary_persona: Neelam Bala’s palate favors authentic regional flavors, regularly preparing sarson da saag, makki di roti, gur‑laden laddoos and herb‑infused tea with mint and coriander, while they enjoy experimenting with seasonal vegetables from their balcony garden.
payload.persona: Neelam Bala blends disciplined household management with a competitive streak, balancing meticulous budgeting and cooking while they occasionally worry‑driven over‑thinking.
payload.cultural_background: Neelam Bala belongs to the Punjabi Hindu community of Jalandhar. Growing up in an urban but tightly‑knit neighbourhood, she has been immersed in the vibrant customs of Punjab – from festive celebrations like Lohri and Diwali to the warmth of joint‑family gatherings. Her cultural upbringing emphasizes respect for elders, a love for Punjabi folk music and dance, and a strong sense of duty towards the household. These influences, combined with the practical, no‑nonsense ethos of city life, shape her preference for orderly routines, dependable habits, and a focus on tangible outcomes.
payload.linguistic_background: Neelam’s mother tongue is Punjabi, spoken in the Gurmukhi script, and she uses it for daily communication with family and neighbours. She is also fluent in Hindi, which she reads and writes using Devanagari, mainly for interacting with the wider community and accessing basic services. Her formal schooling ended before primary level, so her literacy is limited to simple reading and writing in both languages, and she has only a rudimentary grasp of English, sufficient for recognizing common words on product labels.

Every PII/PHI mention in the generated clinical text MUST be wrapped as [[ENTITY_TYPE|surrogate_value]] using an entity id from the allow-list only. Do not emit unlabeled real-looking identifiers. Do not invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER — use MRN / APPOINTMENT_ID / etc.).

- ID-like entity values (Aadhaar, PAN, IFSC, MRN, phone, policy numbers, emails) MAY stay Latin/digits even in Indic documents — that is expected, not impurity.

- VEHICLE_REGISTRATION belongs only in ER arrival, hospital billing, or insurance claim lines — once, never dumped.

- Optional entities may be included when natural for the document type.
- Do not invent entity TYPE names outside the provided allow-list.
- Never invent DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER — write calendar dates/times as plain prose outside tags.

Quality bar (BAD vs GOOD — avoid known judge/auditor failures):
Aim for PERFECT documents. Avoid these known failure modes:

BAD → dialect_script_impurity:
  English (or wrong-script) clinical body when Gujarati/Tamil/Meitei/Ol Chiki/Arabic/… is required.
GOOD:
  Full clinical prose in the assigned language AND exact script. Latin digits only inside ID tags
  (Aadhaar, phone, MRN, IFSC, email). Drug/lab names may stay Latin.

BAD → instruction_drift:
  Assigned domain is oncology but the note narrates generic TB / unrelated complaint.
GOOD:
  Chief complaint, findings, and plan clearly match the assigned clinical domain.

BAD → surrogate_plausibility_collapse:
  Persona district=Faridkot but VILLAGE/HOSPITAL in another state; ABHA local-part unrelated to PATIENT_NAME.
GOOD:
  All places grounded in persona state+district; names and ABHA coherent with PATIENT_NAME.

BAD → domain_persona_mismatch:
  Male persona with maternal delivery; age 70 with paediatric content;
  geriatric / extreme-age routine 28-week pregnancy.
GOOD:
  Sex/age in narrative and tags match persona exactly; maternal content is
  age-plausible (no unsafe extreme late pregnancy as a routine story).

BAD → missing tags / HOSPITAL_ID only:

GOOD:
  [[HOSPITAL_NAME|…]] AND [[HOSPITAL_ID|…]]; [[DOCTOR_NAME|…]] whenever a doctor is mentioned.

BAD → entity stuffing / long SMS:
  AGE/PHONE/EMAIL repeated every chat turn; SMS that is a full discharge dump.
GOOD:
  Each TYPE once (twice max); SMS ≤ ~400 chars with every mandatory tag once.

BAD → invented types / bad checksums:
  [[DATE|…]], [[POLICY_NUMBER|…]]; Aadhaar failing Verhoeff; toy phones like 1234567890.
GOOD:
  Only allow-listed TYPEs (billing: EMAIL→EMAIL_ADDRESS, ADDRESS→RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS;

BAD → localized / translated TYPE names (kills tag protection):
  [[রোগীর_নাম|Anjali]], [[रोगी_नाम|…]], [[PatientName|…]], [[एम.आर.एन|…]].
GOOD:
  Exact ASCII allow-list only: [[PATIENT_NAME|Anjali Mondal]], [[MRN|MRN-…]].
  Translate prose later — NEVER translate the TYPE token.

BAD → repair oscillation (fix A by breaking B):
  Round1 invents DATE to fit a table → Round2 English rewrite to clear invent →

GOOD:
  Fix ALL known issues together: correct ASCII tags + correct domain/persona +
  correct target script (or English pivot if this is the pre-translate stage).

ID-like tagged values stay Latin/digits. Drug names / labs stay untagged.
DOMAIN ANCHOR: chief complaint, findings, and plan MUST match General Medicine — do not default to unrelated TB/generic content unless that is the assigned domain.
GEO ANCHOR: any DISTRICT/VILLAGE/HOSPITAL_NAME must be plausible for persona state=Punjab, district=Jalandhar.
NOTE: translation stage (not this draft) will put the body into Gurmukhi for Punjabi.

Persona anchors (must remain consistent; patient = this persona):
- sex / gender: Female
- age: 29
- marital_status: Never Married
- occupation: No Occupation / Retired / Homemaker
- education_level: Below Primary
- zone: Urban
- state: Punjab
- district: Jalandhar
- first_language: Punjabi
- clinical_persona_summary: A 29-year-old female from Punjab, Jalandhar, who is never married and retired, reports limited education below primary. She lives in an urban zone and speaks Punjabi as her first language, with Hindi proficiency for formal interactions. Clinically, she exhibits stress-related overthinking and disciplined household management, with a history of competitive behavior. Her lifestyle includes daily yoga and walks, and she consumes regional Punjabi cuisine, including sarson da saag and laddoos.

Document format guide for Lab Report (English structure; a later stage translates to the target language):
Example skeleton (English pivot; TYPE names stay ASCII forever):
Lab report — [[HOSPITAL_NAME|Barddhaman District Hospital]] District [[DISTRICT|Purba Bardhaman]]
MRN [[MRN|MRN-WB-2024-0815]] Patient ID [[PATIENT_ID|PID-WB-7781]]
[[PATIENT_NAME|Anjali Mondal]] [[AGE|27]] [[GENDER|Female]] Phone [[PHONE_NUMBER|9876543210]]

NEVER write [[রোগীর_নাম|…]] / [[এম.আর.এন|…]] — only PATIENT_NAME, MRN, etc.

MANDATORY inline entities (9 types) — EACH must appear ≥ once as [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]. Missing ANY is a hard failure:
- PATIENT_NAME — Patient Name e.g. [[PATIENT_NAME|Meena Devi]]

Do NOT invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, TOTAL_AMOUNT, GST_AMOUNT, GRAND_TOTAL). Put dates/times and money amounts in plain prose.

ਮਰੀਜ਼ ਸ਼ਿਕਾਇਤਾਂ ਨਾਲ ਪੇਸ਼ ਹੁੰਦਾ ਹੈ ਪਿਛਲੇ 5 ਦਿਨਾਂ ਤੋਂ ਥਕਾਵਟ, ਹਲਕਾ ਬੁਖਾਰ, ਅਤੇ ਸਰੀਰ ਵਿੱਚ ਦਰਦ। ਇਤਿਹਾਸ ਤੋਂ ਪਤਾ ਲੱਗਦਾ ਹੈ ਘਰੇਲੂ ਜ਼ਿੰਮੇਵਾਰੀਆਂ ਕਾਰਨ ਨਵੀਨਤਮ ਤਣਾਅ। ਕੋਈ ਮਹੱਤਵਪੂਰਨ ਪੁਰਾਣਾ ਡਾਕਟਰੀ ਇਤਿਹਾਸ ਨਹੀਂ ਹੈ। ਸਰੀਰਕ ਜਾਂਚ ਵਿੱਚ ਦਿਖਦਾ ਹੈ ਹਲਕੀ ਪੀਲਾਪਨ, ਤਾਪਮਾਨ 100.8°F, ਬਲੱਡ ਪ੍ਰੈਸ਼ਰ 110/70 mmHg, ਨਾੜੀ ਦੀ ਗਤੀ 88/min, ਸਾਹ ਦੀ ਦਰ 18/min।

ਲੈਬਾਰਟਰੀ ਰਿਪੋਰਟਾਂ:
ਸੰਪੂਰਨ ਖੂਨ ਦੀ ਜਾਂਚ:

- WBC ਗਿਣਤੀ: 7,800/mm³ (ਸਧਾਰਨ)
- RBC ਗਿਣਤੀ: 4.2 million/mm³ (ਸਧਾਰਨ)
- ਪਲੇਟਲੈਟਸ: 2.1 ਲੱਖ/mm³ (ਸਧਾਰਨ)

ਬਾਇਓਕੈਮਿਸਟਰੀ:
- ਖਾਲੀ ਪੇਟ ਖੂਨ ਵਿੱਚ ਸ਼ੂਗਰ: 98 mg/dL (ਸਧਾਰਨ)
- ਕੁੱਲ ਕੋਲੈਸਟ੍ਰੋਲ: 185 mg/dL (ਸਧਾਰਨ)
- ਸੀਰਮ ਸੋਡੀਅਮ: 138 mEq/L (ਸਧਾਰਨ)
- ਸੀਰਮ ਪੋਟਾਸ਼ੀਅਮ: 4.2 mEq/L (ਸਧਾਰਨ)
- ਸੀਰਮ ਕ੍ਰੀਏਟੀਨਾਈਨ: 0.9 mg/dL (ਸਧਾਰਨ)
- ਜਿਗਰ ਦੇ ਕਾਰਜ ਟੈਸਟ: ਸਧਾਰਨ ਸੀਮਾਵਾਂ ਦੇ ਅੰਦਰ

ਪਿਸ਼ਾਬ ਦੀ ਜਾਂਚ:

ਹਲਕਾ ਨੋਰਮੋਸਾਈਟਿਕ ਐਨੀਮੀਆ ਨੋਟ ਕੀਤਾ ਗਿਆ ਹੈ। ਹੋਰ ਪੈਰਾਮੀਟਰ ਸਧਾਰਨ ਸੀਮਾਵਾਂ ਦੇ ਅੰਦਰ ਹਨ। ਕਲੀਨਿਕਲ ਸਬੰਧ ਦੀ ਸਲਾਹ ਦਿੱਤੀ ਜਾਂਦੀ ਹੈ।

ਸਿਫਾਰਸ਼ਾਂ:

Patient presents with complaints of fatigue, mild fever, and generalized body aches for the past 5 days. History reveals recent stress due to household responsibilities. payload.arts_persona: Neelam Bala relaxes by stitching Phulkari embroidery, reads Punjabi short stories by Amrita Pritam, and listens to folk icons like Gurdas Maan and Harbhajan Mann, while they occasionally hum bhajans during chores.

payload.culinary_persona: Neelam Bala’s palate favors authentic regional flavors, regularly preparing sarson da saag, makki di roti, gur‑laden laddoos and herb‑infused tea with mint and coriander, while they enjoy experimenting with seasonal vegetables from their balcony garden.
payload.persona: Neelam Bala blends disciplined household management with a competitive streak, balancing meticulous budgeting and cooking while they occasionally worry‑driven over‑thinking.
payload.cultural_background: Neelam Bala belongs to the Punjabi Hindu community of Jalandhar. Growing up in an urban but tightly‑knit neighbourhood, she has been immersed in the vibrant customs of Punjab – from festive celebrations like Lohri and Diwali to the warmth of joint‑family gatherings. Her cultural upbringing emphasizes respect for elders, a love for Punjabi folk music and dance, and a strong sense of duty towards the household. These influences, combined with the practical, no‑nonsense ethos of city life, shape her preference for orderly routines, dependable habits, and a focus on tangible outcomes.
payload.linguistic_background: Neelam’s mother tongue is Punjabi, spoken in the Gurmukhi script, and she uses it for daily communication with family and neighbours. She is also fluent in Hindi, which she reads and writes using Devanagari, mainly for interacting with the wider community and accessing basic services. Her formal schooling ended before primary level, so her literacy is limited to simple reading and writing in both languages, and she has only a rudimentary grasp of English, sufficient for recognizing common words on product labels.
payload.skills_and_expertise: Neelam is adept at managing a household efficiently, budgeting monthly expenses, and planning meals for her family. She excels in preparing authentic Punjabi dishes such as sarson da saag, makki di roti, and various tiffin items. Her craftsmanship includes traditional Phulkari embroidery and basic sewing, which she occasionally uses for repairing clothing. Despite limited formal schooling, she possesses basic literacy in Punjabi (Gurmukhi) and Hindi (Devanagari) and can handle simple correspondence. She also has rudimentary computer skills, enough to use a basic spreadsheet for tracking household finances.
payload.skills_and_expertise_list: ["Household budgeting", "Punjabi cooking (authentic dishes)", "Traditional Phulkari embroidery", "Basic sewing and mending", "Simple record‑keeping with spreadsheets", "Basic literacy in Punjabi and Hindi", "Organising family events"]
payload.hobbies_and_interests: Neelam enjoys reading Punjabi short stories and poetry, tending to a small balcony garden where she grows herbs and seasonal flowers, experimenting with traditional Punjabi recipes, especially making sweets like gur and laddoo. She practices basic embroidery (Phulkari) as a calming activity and listens to bhajans and kirtans. To manage stress, she does light yoga and meditation. Occasionally, she joins a close‑knit group of friends for chai and gossip, but generally prefers solitary or small‑group activities.
payload.hobbies_and_interests_list: ["Reading Punjabi literature", "Gardening", "Traditional cooking", "Embroidery (Phulkari)", "Listening to devotional music", "Yoga and meditation"]
payload.career_goals_and_ambitions: Neelam's primary career ambition is to transform her household management expertise into a modest home‑based catering service that provides tiffin meals to local office workers. She seeks to complete an adult literacy certification to sharpen her reading, writing, and numeracy, and to acquire basic computer training for efficient bookkeeping. Within the next three to five years, she aims to be recognized for her punctual, high‑quality Punjabi cuisine, expand her client base, and contribute financially to her ageing parents. She also hopes to develop a small network of fellow homemakers for mutual support, reflecting her competitive drive to excel in a niche market.
payload.persona_clinical_summary: A 29-year-old female from Jalandhar, Punjab, who is never married, has below primary education and lives in an urban zone. She speaks Punjabi as her first language and is a homemaker. Clinically, she may exhibit stress-related overthinking and disciplined budgeting, with a preference for regional cuisine and daily physical activity. Her lifestyle includes occasional use of Hindi for written communication.
payload.system_prompt:
You generate synthetic Indian clinical documents with inline surrogate PHI/PII annotations for dataset creation under DPDP-safe research use.

GENERATION STAGE = ENGLISH PIVOT: write clinical prose in English (Latin script). A later pipeline stage translates prose into the target Indic language/script. Do NOT write the document body in Bengali/Hindi/Tamil/etc. at this stage.

CRITICAL — TYPE names are NEVER translated: always exact ASCII uppercase allow-list ids, e.g. [[PATIENT_NAME|Anjali Mondal]] NOT [[রোগীর_নাম|…]] or [[रोगी_नाम|…]] or [[PatientName|…]].

Inline annotation format: [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]
Every PII/PHI mention in the generated clinical text MUST be wrapped as [[ENTITY_TYPE|surrogate_value]] using an entity id from the allow-list only. Do not emit unlabeled real-looking identifiers. Do not invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER — use MRN / APPOINTMENT_ID / etc.).

Rules:
- Use ONLY synthetic surrogate values. Never copy real people or real IDs.
- Keep the same surrogate for the same entity type within one document (DICS).
- At generation (English pivot): write clinical prose in English. TYPE names stay exact ASCII allow-list forever (PATIENT_NAME, MRN, …). NEVER translate or localize the TYPE token itself. A later stage translates prose to the target Indic script.

- ID-like entity values (Aadhaar, PAN, IFSC, MRN, phone, policy numbers, emails) MAY stay Latin/digits even in Indic documents — that is expected, not impurity.

- Use ONLY allow-list TYPE names. Billing aliases: EMAIL/CONTACT_EMAIL → EMAIL_ADDRESS; ADDRESS_NUMBER → RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS; RECEIPT_NUMBER and STATE are NOT types (receipt no. / state stay plain prose; geography uses DISTRICT).

- PAN must match ^[A-Z]{5}[0-9]{4}[A-Z]$ e.g. ABCDE1234F — not KAAPR1234C-style junk.

- Person/place names should prefer the document script when natural; Latin names are OK if culturally plausible.
- Ground geography in the persona state/district/zone when location appears.
- Age and gender in the note MUST match the persona fields exactly.
- Clinical content MUST fit the assigned domain AND the persona (no maternal delivery for male patients; no paediatric domain for adults).
- Maternal_health must be age-plausible: routine ANC/delivery for ages ~18–40; ages 40–42 → high-risk ANC / PNC / contraception — never invent extreme late pregnancy (e.g. 49yo as routine 28-week gestation).

- Include every mandatory entity from the doc-type profile at least once (required ∪ optional when require_all_profile_entities is on).

- Prefer exactly one tag per mandatory TYPE. Never dump/repeat the same TYPE to pad coverage (max 2 occurrences). No trailing entity laundry-lists.

- Device/network IDs (IP_ADDRESS, URL, IMEI_NUMBER, MAC_ADDRESS) belong only in telemedicine session metadata — once each, never inside chat turns.

- VEHICLE_REGISTRATION belongs only in ER arrival, hospital billing, or insurance claim lines — once, never dumped.

- Optional entities may be included when natural for the document type.
- Do not invent entity TYPE names outside the provided allow-list.
- Never invent DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER — write calendar dates/times as plain prose outside tags.

- Do not wrap non-PHI clinical prose (symptoms, meds, vitals, drug names) as entities.
- Never tag currency/amounts (rupees, GST, totals, line-item rates) — leave as plain numbers.
- Use 10-digit Indian mobiles (optional +91 / 91 prefix). Avoid toy patterns.
- Aadhaar must be 12 digits, leading 2–9, Verhoeff-valid (synthetic checksum OK).
- automated_sms must stay short (≤ ~400 characters, 2–4 sentences) — never dump a full chart into an SMS.
- Output ONLY the clinical document text — no markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.

ENTITY TYPE ALLOW-LIST (use ONLY these TYPE names — copy spelling exactly):
AADHAAR_NUMBER, ABHA_ADDRESS, ABHA_ID, ADMISSION_NUMBER, AGE, APPOINTMENT_ID, ASHA_WORKER_NAME, BANK_ACCOUNT_NUMBER, BANK_ROUTING_NUMBER, BED_NUMBER, BPL_RATION_CARD, CASTE, CREDIT_CARD_NUMBER, CVV, DISTRICT, DOB, DOCTOR_NAME, DRIVING_LICENCE, EMAIL_ADDRESS, EMPLOYEE_ID, ENCOUNTER_ID, GENDER, HOSPITAL_ID, HOSPITAL_NAME, IFSC_CODE, IMEI_NUMBER, INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER, IP_ADDRESS, MAC_ADDRESS, MRN, OCCUPATION, PAN_NUMBER, PASSPORT_NUMBER, PATIENT_ID, PATIENT_NAME, PHONE_NUMBER, PIN, PIN_CODE, REFERRAL_ID, RELATIVE_NAME, RELIGION, RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS, STUDENT_ID, TAX_ID, TELEPHONE_LANDLINE, URL, VEHICLE_REGISTRATION, VILLAGE, VOTER_ID, WARD_NUMBER

Format examples (copy the bracket pattern; invent new synthetic values):
  [[AADHAAR_NUMBER|157815659387]]
  [[ABHA_ADDRESS|meena.devi@abdm]]
  [[ABHA_ID|12-3456-7890-1234]]
  [[ADMISSION_NUMBER|ADM-2024-0912]]
  [[AGE|34]]
  [[APPOINTMENT_ID|APT-240521-01]]
  [[ASHA_WORKER_NAME|Sunita Yadav]]
  [[BANK_ACCOUNT_NUMBER|50200012345678]]
  [[BANK_ROUTING_NUMBER|SBIN0001234]]
  [[BED_NUMBER|12]]
  [[BPL_RATION_CARD|BPL-UP-208001-4421]]
  [[CASTE|OBC]]
  [[CREDIT_CARD_NUMBER|4111111111111111]]
  [[CVV|123]]
  [[DISTRICT|Kanpur Nagar]]
  [[DOB|1992-03-14]]
  [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Anjali Sharma]]
  [[DRIVING_LICENCE|UP14 20110012345]]
  [[EMAIL_ADDRESS|meena.devi@example.com]]
  [[EMPLOYEE_ID|EMP-77821]]
  [[ENCOUNTER_ID|ENC-55601]]
  [[GENDER|Female]]
  [[HOSPITAL_ID|HSP-UP-014]]
  [[HOSPITAL_NAME|District Hospital Kanpur]]
  [[IFSC_CODE|SBIN0001234]]
  [[IMEI_NUMBER|356938035643809]]
  [[INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER|POL-PB-2024-7781]]
  [[IP_ADDRESS|103.21.244.12]]
  [[MAC_ADDRESS|00:1A:2B:3C:4D:5E]]
  [[MRN|MRN-2024-0815-001]]
  [[OCCUPATION|Teacher]]
  [[PAN_NUMBER|ABCDE1234F]]
  [[PASSPORT_NUMBER|Z1234567]]
  [[PATIENT_ID|PID-77821]]
  [[PATIENT_NAME|Meena Devi]]
  [[PHONE_NUMBER|9876512340]]
  [[PIN|4829]]
  [[PIN_CODE|208001]]
  [[REFERRAL_ID|REF-2024-1123]]
  [[RELATIVE_NAME|Ramesh Devi]]
  [[RELIGION|Hindu]]
  [[RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS|12 Gandhi Nagar, Ward 4]]
  [[STUDENT_ID|STU-2024-0912]]
  [[TAX_ID|29ABCDE1234F1Z5]]
  [[TELEPHONE_LANDLINE|0512-2345678]]
  [[URL|https://example.org/u/meena]]
  [[VEHICLE_REGISTRATION|UP78AB1234]]
  [[VILLAGE|Shivpur]]
  [[VOTER_ID|ABC1234567]]
  [[WARD_NUMBER|B2]]

Latin/digits OK inside these entity values even for Indic documents: AADHAAR_NUMBER, PAN_NUMBER, PASSPORT_NUMBER, DRIVING_LICENCE, VOTER_ID, BPL_RATION_CARD, TAX_ID, PHONE_NUMBER, TELEPHONE_LANDLINE, EMAIL_ADDRESS, PIN_CODE, IP_ADDRESS, URL, BANK_ACCOUNT_NUMBER, IFSC_CODE, BANK_ROUTING_NUMBER, CREDIT_CARD_NUMBER, CVV, PIN, EMPLOYEE_ID, STUDENT_ID, IMEI_NUMBER, MAC_ADDRESS, VEHICLE_REGISTRATION, ABHA_ID, ABHA_ADDRESS, MRN, PATIENT_ID, HOSPITAL_ID, ADMISSION_NUMBER, ENCOUNTER_ID, APPOINTMENT_ID, REFERRAL_ID, INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER, BED_NUMBER, WARD_NUMBER
Do NOT invent types like DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER. Put calendar dates/times in plain prose.

Quality bar (BAD vs GOOD — avoid known judge/auditor failures):
Aim for PERFECT documents. Avoid these known failure modes:

BAD → dialect_script_impurity:
  English (or wrong-script) clinical body when Gujarati/Tamil/Meitei/Ol Chiki/Arabic/… is required.
GOOD:
  Full clinical prose in the assigned language AND exact script. Latin digits only inside ID tags
  (Aadhaar, phone, MRN, IFSC, email). Drug/lab names may stay Latin.

BAD → instruction_drift:
  Assigned domain is oncology but the note narrates generic TB / unrelated complaint.
GOOD:
  Chief complaint, findings, and plan clearly match the assigned clinical domain.

BAD → surrogate_plausibility_collapse:
  Persona district=Faridkot but VILLAGE/HOSPITAL in another state; ABHA local-part unrelated to PATIENT_NAME.
GOOD:
  All places grounded in persona state+district; names and ABHA coherent with PATIENT_NAME.

BAD → domain_persona_mismatch:
  Male persona with maternal delivery; age 70 with paediatric content;
  geriatric / extreme-age routine 28-week pregnancy.
GOOD:
  Sex/age in narrative and tags match persona exactly; maternal content is
  age-plausible (no unsafe extreme late pregnancy as a routine story).

BAD → missing tags / HOSPITAL_ID only:
  Facility name in prose, only [[HOSPITAL_ID|…]] tagged; doctor name untagged.
GOOD:
  [[HOSPITAL_NAME|…]] AND [[HOSPITAL_ID|…]]; [[DOCTOR_NAME|…]] whenever a doctor is mentioned.

BAD → entity stuffing / long SMS:
  AGE/PHONE/EMAIL repeated every chat turn; SMS that is a full discharge dump.
GOOD:
  Each TYPE once (twice max); SMS ≤ ~400 chars with every mandatory tag once.

BAD → invented types / bad checksums:
  [[DATE|…]], [[POLICY_NUMBER|…]]; Aadhaar failing Verhoeff; toy phones like 1234567890.
GOOD:
  Only allow-listed TYPEs (billing: EMAIL→EMAIL_ADDRESS, ADDRESS→RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS;
  receipt/state/money stay plain prose). Valid-looking synthetic Aadhaar/phone.

BAD → localized / translated TYPE names (kills tag protection):
  [[রোগীর_নাম|Anjali]], [[रोगी_नाम|…]], [[PatientName|…]], [[एम.आर.एन|…]].
GOOD:
  Exact ASCII allow-list only: [[PATIENT_NAME|Anjali Mondal]], [[MRN|MRN-…]].
  Translate prose later — NEVER translate the TYPE token.

BAD → repair oscillation (fix A by breaking B):
  Round1 invents DATE to fit a table → Round2 English rewrite to clear invent →
  Round3 dialect_script_impurity → Round4 invent again.
GOOD:
  Fix ALL known issues together: correct ASCII tags + correct domain/persona +
  correct target script (or English pivot if this is the pre-translate stage).
  Never English-rewrite a finished Indic body just to repair tags.

Language SLA:
  hi/ta/te/bn/mr/… must stay fluent with tags — failures are our prompt/repair bug.
  brx/mni/sat (rare scripts) may soft-fail; do not collapse the whole run for them.
payload.user_prompt:
Write one complete PHC Register Entry (Primary Health Centre) for clinical domain Tuberculosis & Non-Communicable Diseases.

GENERATION LANGUAGE: English (Latin script) — this is an English pivot draft. A later pipeline stage will translate clinical prose into Punjabi (code=pa, script=Gurmukhi). Keep [[TYPE|value]] tags intact and valid.
CRITICAL: TYPE names are NEVER translated or localized — always exact ASCII allow-list (PATIENT_NAME, MRN, …). Forbidden: [[রোগীর_নাম|…]], [[रोगी_नाम|…]], [[PatientName|…]].
ID-like tagged values stay Latin/digits. Drug names / labs stay untagged.
DOMAIN ANCHOR: chief complaint, findings, and plan MUST match Tuberculosis & Non-Communicable Diseases — do not default to unrelated TB/generic content unless that is the assigned domain.
GEO ANCHOR: any DISTRICT/VILLAGE/HOSPITAL_NAME must be plausible for persona state=Punjab, district=Jalandhar.
NOTE: translation stage (not this draft) will put the body into Gurmukhi for Punjabi.

Persona anchors (must remain consistent; patient = this persona):
- sex / gender: Female
- age: 29
- marital_status: Never Married
- occupation: No Occupation / Retired / Homemaker
- education_level: Below Primary
- zone: Urban
- state: Punjab
- district: Jalandhar
- first_language: Punjabi
- clinical_persona_summary: A 29-year-old female from Jalandhar, Punjab, who is never married, has below primary education and lives in an urban zone. She speaks Punjabi as her first language and is a homemaker. Clinically, she may exhibit stress-related overthinking and disciplined budgeting, with a preference for regional cuisine and daily physical activity. Her lifestyle includes occasional use of Hindi for written communication.

Document format guide for PHC Register Entry (Primary Health Centre) (English structure; a later stage translates to the target language):
Example skeleton:
PHC register — [[HOSPITAL_NAME|PHC ...]] Village [[VILLAGE|...]] District [[DISTRICT|...]]
[[PATIENT_NAME|...]] [[AGE|19]] [[GENDER|Female]] | brief entry line

MANDATORY inline entities (11 types) — EACH must appear ≥ once as [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]. Missing ANY is a hard failure:
- PATIENT_NAME — Patient Name e.g. [[PATIENT_NAME|Meena Devi]]
- AGE — Age e.g. [[AGE|34]]
- GENDER — Gender e.g. [[GENDER|Female]]
- VILLAGE — Village e.g. [[VILLAGE|Shivpur]]
- DISTRICT — District e.g. [[DISTRICT|Kanpur Nagar]]
- HOSPITAL_NAME — Hospital / Institution Name e.g. [[HOSPITAL_NAME|District Hospital Kanpur]]
- PHONE_NUMBER — Phone Number e.g. [[PHONE_NUMBER|9876512340]]
- ASHA_WORKER_NAME — ASHA Worker Name e.g. [[ASHA_WORKER_NAME|Sunita Yadav]]
- BPL_RATION_CARD — BPL / Ration Card Number e.g. [[BPL_RATION_CARD|BPL-UP-208001-4421]]
- VOTER_ID — Voter ID e.g. [[VOTER_ID|ABC1234567]]
- ABHA_ID — ABHA ID e.g. [[ABHA_ID|12-3456-7890-1234]]

Before finishing, mentally checklist every mandatory TYPE above.
Do NOT invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, TOTAL_AMOUNT, GST_AMOUNT, GRAND_TOTAL). Put dates/times and money amounts in plain prose.
Produce ONLY the clinical document text with inline annotations. No markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.
payload.generated_text:
PHC ਰਜਿਸਟਰ — [[HOSPITAL_NAME|Primary Health Centre Bhikhiwind]] ਪਿੰਡ [[VILLAGE|Bhikhiwind]] ਜ਼ਿਲ੍ਹਾ [[DISTRICT|Jalandhar]]
[[PATIENT_NAME|Harpreet Kaur]] [[AGE|29]] [[GENDER|Female]] | ਮਰੀਜ਼ ਲਗਾਤਾਰ ਖੰਘ ਅਤੇ ਭਾਰ ਘਟਣ ਦੀ ਜਾਂਚ ਲਈ ਪੇਸ਼ ਹੋਈ।

ਮਰੀਜ਼ 29 ਸਾਲ ਦੀ ਕੁਆਰੀ ਘਰੇਲੂ ਔਰਤ ਹੈ, ਜੋ ਭਿਖੀਵਿੰਡ ਪਿੰਡ ਤੋਂ ਹੈ। ਉਹ ਪਿਛਲੇ ਤਿੰਨ ਹਫ਼ਤਿਆਂ ਤੋਂ ਖੰਘ ਦੀ ਰਿਪੋਰਟ ਕਰਦੀ ਹੈ, ਜੋ ਕਦੇ-ਕਦੇ ਘੱਟ ਬਲਗ਼ਮ ਨਾਲ ਹੁੰਦੀ ਹੈ ਅਤੇ ਸ਼ਾਮ ਨੂੰ ਘੱਟ ਬੁਖ਼ਾਰ ਰਹਿੰਦਾ ਹੈ। ਉਸ ਨੂੰ ਪੂਰੇ ਸਰੀਰ ਵਿੱਚ ਕਮਜ਼ੋਰੀ ਮਹਿਸੂਸ ਹੁੰਦੀ ਹੈ ਅਤੇ ਪਿਛਲੇ ਮਹੀਨੇ ਲਗਭਗ 3 ਕਿਲੋ ਭਾਰ ਘਟਾਇਆ ਹੈ। ਉਹ ਆਪਣੇ ਪਰਿਵਾਰ ਦੀਆਂ ਹਾਲੀਆ ਸਮੱਸਿਆਵਾਂ ਨੂੰ ਤਣਾਅ ਦਾ ਕਾਰਨ ਮੰਨਦੀ ਹੈ। ਉਸ ਦੀ ਪਹਿਲੀ ਭਾਸ਼ਾ ਪੰਜਾਬੀ ਹੈ।

ਜਾਂਚ ਦੌਰਾਨ, ਮਰੀਜ਼ ਦੀ ਹਾਲਤ ਸਥਿਰ ਹੈ। ਬਲੱਡ ਪ੍ਰੈਸ਼ਰ 140/90 mmHg ਦਰਜ ਕੀਤਾ ਗਿਆ ਹੈ। ਸਾਹ ਪ੍ਰਣਾਲੀ ਦੀ ਜਾਂਚ ਵਿੱਚ ਸੱਜੇ ਉੱਪਰਲੇ ਹਿੱਸੇ ਵਿੱਚ ਸਾਹ ਦੀ ਆਵਾਜ਼ ਘੱਟ ਹੈ, ਬਿਨਾਂ ਕਿਸੇ ਸੀਟੀ ਜਾਂ ਕਰੈਕਲਿੰਗ ਦੇ। ਨਹੁੰਆਂ ਦੀ ਸੋਜ ਜਾਂ ਲਿੰਫ ਨੋਡਸ ਦੀ ਸੋਜ ਨਹੀਂ ਦੇਖੀ ਗਈ।

ਜਾਂਚਾਂ ਦੇ ਹੁਕਮ ਦਿੱਤੇ ਗਏ ਹਨ: ਬਲਗ਼ਮ ਦਾ ਨਮੂਨਾ GeneXpert MTB/RIF ਟੈਸਟ ਲਈ ਭੇਜਿਆ ਗਿਆ ਹੈ। CBC ਅਤੇ ਫਾਸਟਿੰਗ ਬਲੱਡ ਸ਼ੂਗਰ ਸਮੇਤ ਬੁਨਿਆਦੀ ਖੂਨ ਦੀ ਜਾਂਚ ਵੀ ਕੀਤੀ ਗਈ ਹੈ।

ਯੋਜਨਾ: ਮਰੀਜ਼ ਨੂੰ ਟੀਬੀ ਦੇ ਇਲਾਜ ਨੂੰ ਪੂਰਾ ਕਰਨ ਦੀ ਮਹੱਤਤਾ ਬਾਰੇ ਸਲਾਹ ਦਿੱਤੀ ਗਈ ਹੈ। ਐਂਟੀ-ਹਾਈਪਰਟੈਂਸਿਵ ਦਵਾਈ (Amlodipine 5mg ਰੋਜ਼ਾਨਾ) ਸ਼ੁਰੂ ਕੀਤੀ ਗਈ ਹੈ। ਉੱਚ-ਪ੍ਰੋਟੀਨ, ਉੱਚ-ਕੈਲੋਰੀ ਵਾਲੀ ਖੁਰਾਕ ਦੀ ਸਲਾਹ ਦਿੱਤੀ ਗਈ ਹੈ। ਸ਼ੁਰੂਆਤੀ ਰਿਪੋਰਟਾਂ ਅਤੇ ਬਲੱਡ ਪ੍ਰੈਸ਼ਰ ਦੀ ਪ੍ਰਤੀਕਿਰਿਆ ਦੀ ਸਮੀਖਿਆ ਕਰਨ ਲਈ ਇੱਕ ਹਫ਼ਤੇ ਵਿੱਚ ਫਾਲੋ-ਅਪ ਮੁਲਾਕਾਤ ਤੈਅ ਕੀਤੀ ਗਈ ਹੈ। [[ASHA_WORKER_NAME|Jaswinder Kaur]] ਨੂੰ ਪਾਲਣਾ ਸਹਾਇਤਾ ਲਈ ਘਰ ਦਾ ਦੌਰਾ ਕਰਨ ਦੀ ਬੇਨਤੀ ਕੀਤੀ ਗਈ ਹੈ। ਮਰੀਜ਼ ਦੇ [[BPL_RATION_CARD|BPL-PB-JAL-2024-00123]] ਅਤੇ [[VOTER_ID|EJ6789012]] ਵੇਰਵਿਆਂ ਦੀ ਲਾਭਾਂ ਲਈ ਪੁਸ਼ਟੀ ਕੀਤੀ ਗਈ ਹੈ। ਉਸ ਦੇ [[PHONE_NUMBER|9876543210]] ਅਤੇ [[ABHA_ID|1234-5678-9012-3456]] ਵੇਰਵੇ ਸਿਸਟਮ ਵਿੱਚ ਅਪਡੇਟ ਕੀਤੇ ਗਏ ਹਨ।
payload.generated_text_en:
PHC register — [[HOSPITAL_NAME|Primary Health Centre Bhikhiwind]] Village [[VILLAGE|Bhikhiwind]] District [[DISTRICT|Jalandhar]]
[[PATIENT_NAME|Harpreet Kaur]] [[AGE|29]] [[GENDER|Female]] | Patient presented for evaluation of persistent cough and weight loss.

Patient is a 29-year-old never-married female homemaker from Bhikhiwind village. She reports a cough for the past three weeks, sometimes productive with scanty sputum, and low-grade evening fevers. She also feels generalized weakness and has lost about 3 kg in the last month. She attributes some stress to recent family issues. Her first language is Punjabi.

On examination, vitals are stable. Blood pressure is recorded at 140/90 mmHg. Respiratory system examination shows reduced breath sounds in the right upper zone, with no wheezes or crepitations. No clubbing or lymphadenopathy is noted.

Investigations ordered: Sputum sample sent for GeneXpert MTB/RIF assay. Basic blood work including CBC and fasting blood sugar is also done.

Plan: Patient counseled on the importance of completing the TB treatment course. Started on anti-hypertensive medication (Amlodipine 5mg daily). Advised a high-protein, high-calorie diet. payload.system_prompt:
You generate synthetic Indian clinical documents with inline surrogate PHI/PII annotations for dataset creation under DPDP-safe research use.

GENERATION STAGE = ENGLISH PIVOT: write clinical prose in English (Latin script). A later pipeline stage translates prose into the target Indic language/script. Do NOT write the document body in Bengali/Hindi/Tamil/etc. at this stage.

CRITICAL — TYPE names are NEVER translated: always exact ASCII uppercase allow-list ids, e.g. [[PATIENT_NAME|Anjali Mondal]] NOT [[রোগীর_নাম|…]] or [[रोगी_नाम|…]] or [[PatientName|…]].

Inline annotation format: [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]
Every PII/PHI mention in the generated clinical text MUST be wrapped as [[ENTITY_TYPE|surrogate_value]] using an entity id from the allow-list only. Do not emit unlabeled real-looking identifiers. Do not invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER — use MRN / APPOINTMENT_ID / etc.).

Rules:
- Use ONLY synthetic surrogate values. Never copy real people or real IDs.
- Keep the same surrogate for the same entity type within one document (DICS).
- At generation (English pivot): write clinical prose in English. TYPE names stay exact ASCII allow-list forever (PATIENT_NAME, MRN, …). NEVER translate or localize the TYPE token itself. A later stage translates prose to the target Indic script.

- ID-like entity values (Aadhaar, PAN, IFSC, MRN, phone, policy numbers, emails) MAY stay Latin/digits even in Indic documents — that is expected, not impurity.

- Use ONLY allow-list TYPE names. Billing aliases: EMAIL/CONTACT_EMAIL → EMAIL_ADDRESS; ADDRESS_NUMBER → RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS; RECEIPT_NUMBER and STATE are NOT types (receipt no. / state stay plain prose; geography uses DISTRICT).

- PAN must match ^[A-Z]{5}[0-9]{4}[A-Z]$ e.g. ABCDE1234F — not KAAPR1234C-style junk.

- Person/place names should prefer the document script when natural; Latin names are OK if culturally plausible.
- Ground geography in the persona state/district/zone when location appears.
- Age and gender in the note MUST match the persona fields exactly.
- Clinical content MUST fit the assigned domain AND the persona (no maternal delivery for male patients; no paediatric domain for adults).
- Maternal_health must be age-plausible: routine ANC/delivery for ages ~18–40; ages 40–42 → high-risk ANC / PNC / contraception — never invent extreme late pregnancy (e.g. 49yo as routine 28-week gestation).

- Include every mandatory entity from the doc-type profile at least once (required ∪ optional when require_all_profile_entities is on).

- Prefer exactly one tag per mandatory TYPE. Never dump/repeat the same TYPE to pad coverage (max 2 occurrences). No trailing entity laundry-lists.

- Device/network IDs (IP_ADDRESS, URL, IMEI_NUMBER, MAC_ADDRESS) belong only in telemedicine session metadata — once each, never inside chat turns.

- VEHICLE_REGISTRATION belongs only in ER arrival, hospital billing, or insurance claim lines — once, never dumped.

- Optional entities may be included when natural for the document type.
- Do not invent entity TYPE names outside the provided allow-list.
- Never invent DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER — write calendar dates/times as plain prose outside tags.

- Do not wrap non-PHI clinical prose (symptoms, meds, vitals, drug names) as entities.
- Never tag currency/amounts (rupees, GST, totals, line-item rates) — leave as plain numbers.
- Use 10-digit Indian mobiles (optional +91 / 91 prefix). Avoid toy patterns.
- Aadhaar must be 12 digits, leading 2–9, Verhoeff-valid (synthetic checksum OK).
- automated_sms must stay short (≤ ~400 characters, 2–4 sentences) — never dump a full chart into an SMS.
- Output ONLY the clinical document text — no markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.

ENTITY TYPE ALLOW-LIST (use ONLY these TYPE names — copy spelling exactly):
AADHAAR_NUMBER, ABHA_ADDRESS, ABHA_ID, ADMISSION_NUMBER, AGE, APPOINTMENT_ID, ASHA_WORKER_NAME, BANK_ACCOUNT_NUMBER, BANK_ROUTING_NUMBER, BED_NUMBER, BPL_RATION_CARD, CASTE, CREDIT_CARD_NUMBER, CVV, DISTRICT, DOB, DOCTOR_NAME, DRIVING_LICENCE, EMAIL_ADDRESS, EMPLOYEE_ID, ENCOUNTER_ID, GENDER, HOSPITAL_ID, HOSPITAL_NAME, IFSC_CODE, IMEI_NUMBER, INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER, IP_ADDRESS, MAC_ADDRESS, MRN, OCCUPATION, PAN_NUMBER, PASSPORT_NUMBER, PATIENT_ID, PATIENT_NAME, PHONE_NUMBER, PIN, PIN_CODE, REFERRAL_ID, RELATIVE_NAME, RELIGION, RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS, STUDENT_ID, TAX_ID, TELEPHONE_LANDLINE, URL, VEHICLE_REGISTRATION, VILLAGE, VOTER_ID, WARD_NUMBER

Do NOT invent types like DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER. Put calendar dates/times in plain prose.

Quality bar (BAD vs GOOD — avoid known judge/auditor failures):
Aim for PERFECT documents. Avoid these known failure modes:

BAD → dialect_script_impurity:
  English (or wrong-script) clinical body when Gujarati/Tamil/Meitei/Ol Chiki/Arabic/… is required.
GOOD:
  Full clinical prose in the assigned language AND exact script. Latin digits only inside ID tags
  (Aadhaar, phone, MRN, IFSC, email). Drug/lab names may stay Latin.

BAD → instruction_drift:
  Assigned domain is oncology but the note narrates generic TB / unrelated complaint.
GOOD:
  Chief complaint, findings, and plan clearly match the assigned clinical domain.

BAD → surrogate_plausibility_collapse:
  Persona district=Faridkot but VILLAGE/HOSPITAL in another state; ABHA local-part unrelated to PATIENT_NAME.
GOOD:
  All places grounded in persona state+district; names and ABHA coherent with PATIENT_NAME.

BAD → domain_persona_mismatch:
  Male persona with maternal delivery; age 70 with paediatric content;
  geriatric / extreme-age routine 28-week pregnancy.
GOOD:
  Sex/age in narrative and tags match persona exactly; maternal content is
  age-plausible (no unsafe extreme late pregnancy as a routine story).

BAD → missing tags / HOSPITAL_ID only:
  Facility name in prose, only [[HOSPITAL_ID|…]] tagged; doctor name untagged.
GOOD:
  [[HOSPITAL_NAME|…]] AND [[HOSPITAL_ID|…]]; [[DOCTOR_NAME|…]] whenever a doctor is mentioned.

BAD → entity stuffing / long SMS:
  AGE/PHONE/EMAIL repeated every chat turn; SMS that is a full discharge dump.
GOOD:
  Each TYPE once (twice max); SMS ≤ ~400 chars with every mandatory tag once.

BAD → invented types / bad checksums:
  [[DATE|…]], [[POLICY_NUMBER|…]]; Aadhaar failing Verhoeff; toy phones like 1234567890.
GOOD:
  Only allow-listed TYPEs (billing: EMAIL→EMAIL_ADDRESS, ADDRESS→RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS;
  receipt/state/money stay plain prose). Valid-looking synthetic Aadhaar/phone.

BAD → localized / translated TYPE names (kills tag protection):
  [[রোগীর_নাম|Anjali]], [[रोगी_नाम|…]], [[PatientName|…]], [[एम.आर.एन|…]].
GOOD:
  Exact ASCII allow-list only: [[PATIENT_NAME|Anjali Mondal]], [[MRN|MRN-…]].
  Translate prose later — NEVER translate the TYPE token.

BAD → repair oscillation (fix A by breaking B):
  Round1 invents DATE to fit a table → Round2 English rewrite to clear invent →
  Round3 dialect_script_impurity → Round4 invent again.
GOOD:
  Fix ALL known issues together: correct ASCII tags + correct domain/persona +
  correct target script (or English pivot if this is the pre-translate stage).
  Never English-rewrite a finished Indic body just to repair tags.

Language SLA:
  hi/ta/te/bn/mr/… must stay fluent with tags — failures are our prompt/repair bug.
  brx/mni/sat (rare scripts) may soft-fail; do not collapse the whole run for them.

ID-like tagged values stay Latin/digits. Drug names / labs stay untagged.
DOMAIN ANCHOR: chief complaint, findings, and plan MUST match Surgical — do not default to unrelated TB/generic content unless that is the assigned domain.
GEO ANCHOR: any DISTRICT/VILLAGE/HOSPITAL_NAME must be plausible for persona state=Rajasthan, district=Bundi.
NOTE: translation stage (not this draft) will put the body into Devanagari for Sanskrit.

- sex / gender: Female
- age: 19
- marital_status: Never Married
- occupation: No Occupation / Retired / Homemaker
- education_level: Higher Secondary/Intermediate Pre-University/Senior Secondary
- zone: Urban

Her cultural background includes Rajput heritage and participation in local festivals like Gangaur and Teej. Clinically, she may have a disciplined but occasionally disorganized daily routine.

Example skeleton:
TPA claim — Policy [[INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER|POL-PB-2024-7781]]
[[PATIENT_NAME|...]] [[AGE|25]] [[GENDER|Male]] Aadhaar [[AADHAAR_NUMBER|157815659387]]

PAN [[PAN_NUMBER|ABCDE1234F]] IFSC [[IFSC_CODE|SBIN0001234]] account [[BANK_ACCOUNT_NUMBER|...]]

- PATIENT_NAME — Patient Name e.g. [[PATIENT_NAME|Meena Devi]]
- AGE — Age e.g. [[AGE|34]]

- PAN_NUMBER — PAN Number e.g. [[PAN_NUMBER|ABCDE1234F]]
- BANK_ACCOUNT_NUMBER — Bank Account Number e.g. [[BANK_ACCOUNT_NUMBER|50200012345678]]
- IFSC_CODE — IFSC Code e.g. [[IFSC_CODE|SBIN0001234]]
- BANK_ROUTING_NUMBER — Bank Routing Number e.g. [[BANK_ROUTING_NUMBER|SBIN0001234]]

- CVV — CVV e.g. [[CVV|123]]
- PIN — PIN e.g. [[PIN|4829]]
- PHONE_NUMBER — Phone Number e.g. [[PHONE_NUMBER|9876512340]]
- VEHICLE_REGISTRATION — Vehicle Registration Number e.g. [[VEHICLE_REGISTRATION|UP78AB1234]]

Before finishing, mentally checklist every mandatory TYPE above.
Do NOT invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, TOTAL_AMOUNT, GST_AMOUNT, GRAND_TOTAL). Put dates/times and money amounts in plain prose.

रोगी द्वि-दिवसीय-तीव्र-उदरशूलः च वमनम् उपस्थापितः अभवत्। परीक्षणे, दक्षिण-अधो-चतुर्थांशे कोमलता रक्षणेन सह दृष्टा। अल्ट्रासाउण्ड्-परीक्षणेन संशयित-अपेंडिसाइटिस्-रोगः प्रकटितः। रोगी 15 मई 2024 दिनाङ्के सामान्य-संज्ञाहरणे अधिनिर्धारित-अपेंडिसेक्टोमी-प्रक्रियाम् अकरोत्। लॅपरोस्कोपिक-पद्धत्या प्रक्रिया सफला अभवत्। शल्यक्रिया-पश्चात् स्वास्थ्य-लाभः निर्विघ्नः आसीत्। रोगी 17 मई 2024 दिनाङ्के एण्टीबायोटिक्स् तथा एनाल्जेसिक-औषधैः सह मुक्तः अभवत्। 7 दिनेषु अनुवर्तनं सूचितम्। अनुमानित-कुल-व्ययः: 45,000 रूप्यकाणि, यत्र शल्यक्रिया, संज्ञाहरण, चिकित्सालय-वासः, औषधानि च सम्मिलितानि सन्ति। नीति-शर्तानां अनुसारं बीमा-व्याप्तिः प्रयोक्तव्या अस्ति।

payload.system_prompt:
You generate synthetic Indian clinical documents with inline surrogate PHI/PII annotations for dataset creation under DPDP-safe research use.

GENERATION STAGE = ENGLISH PIVOT: write clinical prose in English (Latin script). A later pipeline stage translates prose into the target Indic language/script. Do NOT write the document body in Bengali/Hindi/Tamil/etc. at this stage.

CRITICAL — TYPE names are NEVER translated: always exact ASCII uppercase allow-list ids, e.g. [[PATIENT_NAME|Anjali Mondal]] NOT [[রোগীর_নাম|…]] or [[रोगी_नाम|…]] or [[PatientName|…]].

Inline annotation format: [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]
Every PII/PHI mention in the generated clinical text MUST be wrapped as [[ENTITY_TYPE|surrogate_value]] using an entity id from the allow-list only. Do not emit unlabeled real-looking identifiers. Do not invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER — use MRN / APPOINTMENT_ID / etc.).

Rules:
- Use ONLY synthetic surrogate values. Never copy real people or real IDs.
- Keep the same surrogate for the same entity type within one document (DICS).
- At generation (English pivot): write clinical prose in English. TYPE names stay exact ASCII allow-list forever (PATIENT_NAME, MRN, …). NEVER translate or localize the TYPE token itself. A later stage translates prose to the target Indic script.

- ID-like entity values (Aadhaar, PAN, IFSC, MRN, phone, policy numbers, emails) MAY stay Latin/digits even in Indic documents — that is expected, not impurity.

- Use ONLY allow-list TYPE names. Billing aliases: EMAIL/CONTACT_EMAIL → EMAIL_ADDRESS; ADDRESS_NUMBER → RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS; RECEIPT_NUMBER and STATE are NOT types (receipt no. / state stay plain prose; geography uses DISTRICT).

- PAN must match ^[A-Z]{5}[0-9]{4}[A-Z]$ e.g. ABCDE1234F — not KAAPR1234C-style junk.

- Person/place names should prefer the document script when natural; Latin names are OK if culturally plausible.
- Ground geography in the persona state/district/zone when location appears.
- Age and gender in the note MUST match the persona fields exactly.
- Clinical content MUST fit the assigned domain AND the persona (no maternal delivery for male patients; no paediatric domain for adults).
- Maternal_health must be age-plausible: routine ANC/delivery for ages ~18–40; ages 40–42 → high-risk ANC / PNC / contraception — never invent extreme late pregnancy (e.g. 49yo as routine 28-week gestation).

- Include every mandatory entity from the doc-type profile at least once (required ∪ optional when require_all_profile_entities is on).

- Prefer exactly one tag per mandatory TYPE. Never dump/repeat the same TYPE to pad coverage (max 2 occurrences). No trailing entity laundry-lists.

- Device/network IDs (IP_ADDRESS, URL, IMEI_NUMBER, MAC_ADDRESS) belong only in telemedicine session metadata — once each, never inside chat turns.

- VEHICLE_REGISTRATION belongs only in ER arrival, hospital billing, or insurance claim lines — once, never dumped.

- Optional entities may be included when natural for the document type.
- Do not invent entity TYPE names outside the provided allow-list.
- Never invent DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER — write calendar dates/times as plain prose outside tags.

- Do not wrap non-PHI clinical prose (symptoms, meds, vitals, drug names) as entities.
- Never tag currency/amounts (rupees, GST, totals, line-item rates) — leave as plain numbers.
- Use 10-digit Indian mobiles (optional +91 / 91 prefix). Avoid toy patterns.
- Aadhaar must be 12 digits, leading 2–9, Verhoeff-valid (synthetic checksum OK).
- automated_sms must stay short (≤ ~400 characters, 2–4 sentences) — never dump a full chart into an SMS.
- Output ONLY the clinical document text — no markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.

ENTITY TYPE ALLOW-LIST (use ONLY these TYPE names — copy spelling exactly):
AADHAAR_NUMBER, ABHA_ADDRESS, ABHA_ID, ADMISSION_NUMBER, AGE, APPOINTMENT_ID, ASHA_WORKER_NAME, BANK_ACCOUNT_NUMBER, BANK_ROUTING_NUMBER, BED_NUMBER, BPL_RATION_CARD, CASTE, CREDIT_CARD_NUMBER, CVV, DISTRICT, DOB, DOCTOR_NAME, DRIVING_LICENCE, EMAIL_ADDRESS, EMPLOYEE_ID, ENCOUNTER_ID, GENDER, HOSPITAL_ID, HOSPITAL_NAME, IFSC_CODE, IMEI_NUMBER, INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER, IP_ADDRESS, MAC_ADDRESS, MRN, OCCUPATION, PAN_NUMBER, PASSPORT_NUMBER, PATIENT_ID, PATIENT_NAME, PHONE_NUMBER, PIN, PIN_CODE, REFERRAL_ID, RELATIVE_NAME, RELIGION, RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS, STUDENT_ID, TAX_ID, TELEPHONE_LANDLINE, URL, VEHICLE_REGISTRATION, VILLAGE, VOTER_ID, WARD_NUMBER

Format examples (copy the bracket pattern; invent new synthetic values):

  [[ABHA_ID|12-3456-7890-1234]]

  [[EMPLOYEE_ID|EMP-77821]]

  [[HOSPITAL_ID|HSP-UP-014]]

  [[IMEI_NUMBER|356938035643809]]

  [[VEHICLE_REGISTRATION|UP78AB1234]]

Latin/digits OK inside these entity values even for Indic documents: AADHAAR_NUMBER, PAN_NUMBER, PASSPORT_NUMBER, DRIVING_LICENCE, VOTER_ID, BPL_RATION_CARD, TAX_ID, PHONE_NUMBER, TELEPHONE_LANDLINE, EMAIL_ADDRESS, PIN_CODE, IP_ADDRESS, URL, BANK_ACCOUNT_NUMBER, IFSC_CODE, BANK_ROUTING_NUMBER, CREDIT_CARD_NUMBER, CVV, PIN, EMPLOYEE_ID, STUDENT_ID, IMEI_NUMBER, MAC_ADDRESS, VEHICLE_REGISTRATION, ABHA_ID, ABHA_ADDRESS, MRN, PATIENT_ID, HOSPITAL_ID, ADMISSION_NUMBER, ENCOUNTER_ID, APPOINTMENT_ID, REFERRAL_ID, INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER, BED_NUMBER, WARD_NUMBER
Do NOT invent types like DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER. Put calendar dates/times in plain prose.

Quality bar (BAD vs GOOD — avoid known judge/auditor failures):
Aim for PERFECT documents. Avoid these known failure modes:

BAD → dialect_script_impurity:
  English (or wrong-script) clinical body when Gujarati/Tamil/Meitei/Ol Chiki/Arabic/… is required.
GOOD:
  Full clinical prose in the assigned language AND exact script. Latin digits only inside ID tags
  (Aadhaar, phone, MRN, IFSC, email). Drug/lab names may stay Latin.

BAD → instruction_drift:
  Assigned domain is oncology but the note narrates generic TB / unrelated complaint.
GOOD:
  Chief complaint, findings, and plan clearly match the assigned clinical domain.

BAD → surrogate_plausibility_collapse:
  Persona district=Faridkot but VILLAGE/HOSPITAL in another state; ABHA local-part unrelated to PATIENT_NAME.
GOOD:
  All places grounded in persona state+district; names and ABHA coherent with PATIENT_NAME.

BAD → domain_persona_mismatch:
  Male persona with maternal delivery; age 70 with paediatric content;
  geriatric / extreme-age routine 28-week pregnancy.
GOOD:
  Sex/age in narrative and tags match persona exactly; maternal content is
  age-plausible (no unsafe extreme late pregnancy as a routine story).

BAD → missing tags / HOSPITAL_ID only:
  Facility name in prose, only [[HOSPITAL_ID|…]] tagged; doctor name untagged.
GOOD:
  [[HOSPITAL_NAME|…]] AND [[HOSPITAL_ID|…]]; [[DOCTOR_NAME|…]] whenever a doctor is mentioned.

BAD → entity stuffing / long SMS:
  AGE/PHONE/EMAIL repeated every chat turn; SMS that is a full discharge dump.
GOOD:
  Each TYPE once (twice max); SMS ≤ ~400 chars with every mandatory tag once.

BAD → invented types / bad checksums:
  [[DATE|…]], [[POLICY_NUMBER|…]]; Aadhaar failing Verhoeff; toy phones like 1234567890.
GOOD:
  Only allow-listed TYPEs (billing: EMAIL→EMAIL_ADDRESS, ADDRESS→RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS;
  receipt/state/money stay plain prose). Valid-looking synthetic Aadhaar/phone.

BAD → localized / translated TYPE names (kills tag protection):
  [[রোগীর_নাম|Anjali]], [[रोगी_नाम|…]], [[PatientName|…]], [[एम.आर.एन|…]].
GOOD:
  Exact ASCII allow-list only: [[PATIENT_NAME|Anjali Mondal]], [[MRN|MRN-…]].
  Translate prose later — NEVER translate the TYPE token.

BAD → repair oscillation (fix A by breaking B):
  Round1 invents DATE to fit a table → Round2 English rewrite to clear invent →
  Round3 dialect_script_impurity → Round4 invent again.
GOOD:
  Fix ALL known issues together: correct ASCII tags + correct domain/persona +
  correct target script (or English pivot if this is the pre-translate stage).
  Never English-rewrite a finished Indic body just to repair tags.

Language SLA:
  hi/ta/te/bn/mr/… must stay fluent with tags — failures are our prompt/repair bug.
  brx/mni/sat (rare scripts) may soft-fail; do not collapse the whole run for them.
payload.user_prompt:
Write one complete Prescription for clinical domain Tuberculosis & Non-Communicable Diseases.

GENERATION LANGUAGE: English (Latin script) — this is an English pivot draft. A later pipeline stage will translate clinical prose into Sanskrit (code=sa, script=Devanagari). Keep [[TYPE|value]] tags intact and valid.
CRITICAL: TYPE names are NEVER translated or localized — always exact ASCII allow-list (PATIENT_NAME, MRN, …). Forbidden: [[রোগীর_নাম|…]], [[रोगी_नाम|…]], [[PatientName|…]].
ID-like tagged values stay Latin/digits. Drug names / labs stay untagged.
DOMAIN ANCHOR: chief complaint, findings, and plan MUST match Tuberculosis & Non-Communicable Diseases — do not default to unrelated TB/generic content unless that is the assigned domain.
GEO ANCHOR: any DISTRICT/VILLAGE/HOSPITAL_NAME must be plausible for persona state=Rajasthan, district=Bundi.
NOTE: translation stage (not this draft) will put the body into Devanagari for Sanskrit.

Persona anchors (must remain consistent; patient = this persona):
- sex / gender: Female
- age: 19
- marital_status: Never Married
- occupation: No Occupation / Retired / Homemaker
- education_level: Higher Secondary/Intermediate Pre-University/Senior Secondary
- zone: Urban
- state: Rajasthan
- district: Bundi
- first_language: Sanskrit
- clinical_persona_summary: A 19-year-old female from Bundi, Rajasthan, who is a retired homemaker with higher secondary education, speaks Sanskrit, Hindi, and English, and is never married. She resides in an urban zone and maintains a routine-driven lifestyle centered on traditional studies and household management. Her cultural background includes Rajput heritage and participation in local festivals like Gangaur and Teej. Clinically, she may have limited physical activity but engages in yoga and walking.

Document format guide for Prescription (English structure; a later stage translates to the target language):
Example skeleton:
Prescription — [[HOSPITAL_NAME|...]]
Patient [[PATIENT_NAME|...]], [[AGE|28]] / [[GENDER|Male]], MRN [[MRN|RX-1001]]
Dr. [[DOCTOR_NAME|...]]
Rx: <drug dose frequency>  (do NOT tag drug names as entities)

MANDATORY inline entities (11 types) — EACH must appear ≥ once as [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]. Missing ANY is a hard failure:
- PATIENT_NAME — Patient Name e.g. [[PATIENT_NAME|Meena Devi]]
- AGE — Age e.g. [[AGE|34]]
- GENDER — Gender e.g. [[GENDER|Female]]
- DOCTOR_NAME — Doctor Name e.g. [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Anjali Sharma]]
- HOSPITAL_NAME — Hospital / Institution Name e.g. [[HOSPITAL_NAME|District Hospital Kanpur]]
- MRN — Medical Record Number (MRN) e.g. [[MRN|MRN-2024-0815-001]]
- PHONE_NUMBER — Phone Number e.g. [[PHONE_NUMBER|9876512340]]
- DISTRICT — District e.g. [[DISTRICT|Kanpur Nagar]]
- RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS — Residential Address e.g. [[RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS|12 Gandhi Nagar, Ward 4]]
- PATIENT_ID — Patient ID e.g. [[PATIENT_ID|PID-77821]]
- ABHA_ID — ABHA ID e.g. [[ABHA_ID|12-3456-7890-1234]]

Before finishing, mentally checklist every mandatory TYPE above.
Do NOT invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, TOTAL_AMOUNT, GST_AMOUNT, GRAND_TOTAL). Put dates/times and money amounts in plain prose.
Produce ONLY the clinical document text with inline annotations. No markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.

चिकित्सा-विधिः:
- इसोनिआज़िड् 300mg प्रतिदिनम् एकवारम्
- रिफाम्पिसिन् 450mg प्रतिदिनम् एकवारम्

परामर्शः: पूर्ण-पाठ्यक्रमं पूर्णं कुर्वन्तु, कस्यापि पार्श्व-प्रभावस्य सूचनां तत्क्षणं ददतु।
payload.generated_text_en:
Prescription — [[HOSPITAL_NAME|Bundi District Hospital]]
Patient [[PATIENT_NAME|Kavita Kumari]], [[AGE|19]] / [[GENDER|Female]], MRN [[MRN|BUN-2024-0012]]
Dr. [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Rajesh Sharma]]
Phone: [[PHONE_NUMBER|9414012345]]
ABHA ID: [[ABHA_ID|12-3456-7890-1234]]
Patient ID: [[PATIENT_ID|PID-BUN-2024-0012]]
District: [[DISTRICT|Bundi]]
Address: [[RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS|15 Nehru Colony, Ward 12, Bundi]]

Chief Complaint: Persistent cough for 3 weeks with low-grade fever and weight loss of 2 kg.

Diagnosis: Pulmonary Tuberculosis (Category I) with iron deficiency anemia.

Treatment Regimen:
- Isoniazid 300mg once daily
- Rifampicin 450mg once daily  
- Pyrazinamide 750mg once daily
- Ethambutol 750mg once daily
For 2 months (intensive phase)

Followed by:
- Isoniazid 300mg once daily
- Rifampicin 450mg once daily
For 4 months (continuation phase)

Investigations: Sputum for AFB, CBC, ESR, Liver function tests, Chest X-ray.

Follow-up: Weekly DOTS observation at Primary Health Center, Bundi.

Diet: High protein diet, iron supplements as prescribed.

Counseling: Complete full course, report any side effects immediately.

payload.arts_persona: Malti Odia, a 59‑year‑old folk song enthusiast, routinely sings traditional ballads like ‘Phula Rachana’, dances spiritedly at festivals, listens avidly to elder storytellers of mythological epics, and draws artistic inspiration from performers such as the renowned Odissi dancer Sonal Mansingh and folk vocalist Gopal Chandra.
payload.travel_persona: Malti Odia, a 59‑year‑old modest traveler, enjoys short trips to nearby Puri during Rath Yatra, visits Bhubaneswar’s weekly market for spices, dreams of a pilgrimage to Varanasi and a cultural tour of Kolkata, but plans each journey carefully within their limited savings and family responsibilities.
payload.culinary_persona: Malti Odia, a 59‑year‑old culinary aficionado, delights in preparing and savoring traditional dishes such as pakhala with fermented rice, tangy fish curry with mustard seeds, sweet chhena poda, arisa, and soft rasgulla, and prefers ingredients like mustard, poppy seeds and fresh river fish, often sharing these meals with family and neighbors during festivals.
payload.persona: Malti Odia, a 59‑year‑old village matriarch, blends bright folk singing, deft handloom weaving, and herbal home remedies with a perpetual curiosity, compassionate teamwork, and a disciplined savings habit, all while juggling limited literacy and a lively social circle.
payload.cultural_background: Malti grew up in the rural village of Nabarangapur in the southern part of Odisha, where Odia culture is deeply rooted in agrarian life, tribal influences, and Hindu traditions. Her family follows the customs of joint households, respects elders, and participates in regional festivals such as Raja Parba, Makar Sankranti, and the grand Rath Yatra of Puri. The everyday rhythm of village life, from morning prayers at the home shrine to communal feasts, shapes her sense of belonging and cultural identity.
payload.linguistic_background: Malti's mother tongue is Odia, which she speaks fluently and uses for all daily communication. Because she never attended school, she cannot read or write in any language, including Odia or Hindi. She can understand basic Hindi spoken on radio and television, and she occasionally picks up simple English words like 'school' or 'doctor' from community interactions, but she does not converse in English. Her linguistic competence is thus strong oral Odia, limited comprehension of Hindi, and no literacy.
payload.skills_and_expertise: Malti excels at traditional Odia cooking, especially preparing regional dishes like pakhala, rasgulla, and fish curries. She is skilled in handloom weaving, creating sarees and scarves for her family. Her expertise includes efficient home management, organizing community events, using herbal remedies for common ailments, maintaining a small vegetable garden, and preserving folk songs and stories by narrating them to younger generations.

payload.hobbies_and_interests: Malti enjoys singing traditional Odia folk songs during evenings, dancing at festivals, tending to her vegetable garden, preparing sweets like arisa and chhena poda, attending village fairs, and listening to elders recount mythological stories.
payload.hobbies_and_interests_list: ['Folk singing', 'Festive dancing', 'Gardening', 'Traditional sweet making', 'Attending village fairs', 'Listening to mythological stories']
payload.career_goals_and_ambitions: Malti hopes to pass her culinary and weaving knowledge to her grandchildren, ensuring they appreciate Odia heritage. She wishes to support local women's self-help groups by sharing her expertise in household budgeting and traditional crafts. Additionally, she aspires to learn basic reading and writing in Odia through community literacy programs, so she can keep records of family recipes and festival schedules.

She is illiterate, speaks only Odia, and has limited exposure to formal healthcare. Her lifestyle includes traditional herbal remedies and a diet rich in local staples like pakhala and chhena poda. She resides in a joint family setting and has limited mobility.

Quality bar (BAD vs GOOD — avoid known judge/auditor failures):
Aim for PERFECT documents. Avoid these known failure modes:

BAD → dialect_script_impurity:
  English (or wrong-script) clinical body when Gujarati/Tamil/Meitei/Ol Chiki/Arabic/… is required.
GOOD:
  Full clinical prose in the assigned language AND exact script. Latin digits only inside ID tags
  (Aadhaar, phone, MRN, IFSC, email). Drug/lab names may stay Latin.

BAD → instruction_drift:
  Assigned domain is oncology but the note narrates generic TB / unrelated complaint.
GOOD:
  Chief complaint, findings, and plan clearly match the assigned clinical domain.

BAD → surrogate_plausibility_collapse:
  Persona district=Faridkot but VILLAGE/HOSPITAL in another state; ABHA local-part unrelated to PATIENT_NAME.
GOOD:
  All places grounded in persona state+district; names and ABHA coherent with PATIENT_NAME.

BAD → domain_persona_mismatch:
  Male persona with maternal delivery; age 70 with paediatric content;
  geriatric / extreme-age routine 28-week pregnancy.

  Sex/age in narrative and tags match persona exactly; maternal content is
  age-plausible (no unsafe extreme late pregnancy as a routine story).

BAD → missing tags / HOSPITAL_ID only:
  Facility name in prose, only [[HOSPITAL_ID|…]] tagged; doctor name untagged.
GOOD:
  [[HOSPITAL_NAME|…]] AND [[HOSPITAL_ID|…]]; [[DOCTOR_NAME|…]] whenever a doctor is mentioned.

BAD → entity stuffing / long SMS:
  AGE/PHONE/EMAIL repeated every chat turn; SMS that is a full discharge dump.
GOOD:
  Each TYPE once (twice max); SMS ≤ ~400 chars with every mandatory tag once.

BAD → invented types / bad checksums:
  [[DATE|…]], [[POLICY_NUMBER|…]]; Aadhaar failing Verhoeff; toy phones like 1234567890.
GOOD:
  Only allow-listed TYPEs (billing: EMAIL→EMAIL_ADDRESS, ADDRESS→RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS;
  receipt/state/money stay plain prose). Valid-looking synthetic Aadhaar/phone.

BAD → localized / translated TYPE names (kills tag protection):
  [[রোগীর_নাম|Anjali]], [[रोगी_नाम|…]], [[PatientName|…]], [[एम.आर.एन|…]].
GOOD:
  Exact ASCII allow-list only: [[PATIENT_NAME|Anjali Mondal]], [[MRN|MRN-…]].
  Translate prose later — NEVER translate the TYPE token.

BAD → repair oscillation (fix A by breaking B):
  Round1 invents DATE to fit a table → Round2 English rewrite to clear invent →
  Round3 dialect_script_impurity → Round4 invent again.
GOOD:
  Fix ALL known issues together: correct ASCII tags + correct domain/persona +
  correct target script (or English pivot if this is the pre-translate stage).
  Never English-rewrite a finished Indic body just to repair tags.

Language SLA:
  hi/ta/te/bn/mr/… must stay fluent with tags — failures are our prompt/repair bug.
  brx/mni/sat (rare scripts) may soft-fail; do not collapse the whole run for them.

payload.generated_text_en:
Operative note — [[HOSPITAL_NAME|District Hospital Nabarangapur]] Adm [[ADMISSION_NUMBER|ADM-2024-1123]] Ward [[WARD_NUMBER|OT]]
[[PATIENT_NAME|Malti Devi]] [[AGE|59]] [[GENDER|Female]] Surgeon [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Ramesh Patnaik]]
Procedure / findings: Under general anesthesia, patient underwent right cervical lymph node excision for suspected tuberculous lymphadenitis. Pre-operative investigations revealed elevated ESR and positive Mantoux test. Intra-operative findings showed multiple matted lymph nodes with caseous necrosis. Tissue sent for histopathology and AFB staining. Procedure completed successfully with minimal blood loss. Post-operative antibiotics initiated. Patient transferred to [[BED_NUMBER|15]] in [[WARD_NUMBER|B3]] for observation. [[MRN|MRN-2024-1123-001]] [[PATIENT_ID|PID-98765]] [[RELATIVE_NAME|Sushil Odia]] spouse informed about procedure and post-operative care instructions provided. Follow-up scheduled in 2 weeks.

She can accurately recite and interpret Sanskrit shlokas, prepare authentic Rajasthani dishes such as dal‑baati‑churma, hand‑embroider traditional patterns, keep meticulous household accounts, and guide family members in basic yoga and meditation practices. BAD → dialect_script_impurity:

GOOD:

BAD → instruction_drift:

BAD → surrogate_plausibility_collapse:
  Persona district=Faridkot but VILLAGE/HOSPITAL in another state; ABHA local-part unrelated to PATIENT_NAME.

BAD → missing tags / HOSPITAL_ID only:

GOOD:
  [[HOSPITAL_NAME|…]] AND [[HOSPITAL_ID|…]]; [[DOCTOR_NAME|…]] whenever a doctor is mentioned.

BAD → entity stuffing / long SMS:
  AGE/PHONE/EMAIL repeated every chat turn; SMS that is a full discharge dump.
GOOD:
  Each TYPE once (twice max); SMS ≤ ~400 chars with every mandatory tag once.

BAD → invented types / bad checksums:
  [[DATE|…]], [[POLICY_NUMBER|…]]; Aadhaar failing Verhoeff; toy phones like 1234567890.
GOOD:
  Only allow-listed TYPEs (billing: EMAIL→EMAIL_ADDRESS, ADDRESS→RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS;
  receipt/state/money stay plain prose). Valid-looking synthetic Aadhaar/phone.

BAD → localized / translated TYPE names (kills tag protection):
  [[রোগীর_নাম|Anjali]], [[रोगी_नाम|…]], [[PatientName|…]], [[एम.आर.एन|…]].
GOOD:
  Exact ASCII allow-list only: [[PATIENT_NAME|Anjali Mondal]], [[MRN|MRN-…]].
  Translate prose later — NEVER translate the TYPE token.

BAD → repair oscillation (fix A by breaking B):
  Round1 invents DATE to fit a table → Round2 English rewrite to clear invent →
  Round3 dialect_script_impurity → Round4 invent again.
GOOD:
  Fix ALL known issues together: correct ASCII tags + correct domain/persona +
  correct target script (or English pivot if this is the pre-translate stage).
  Never English-rewrite a finished Indic body just to repair tags.

Language SLA:
  hi/ta/te/bn/mr/… must stay fluent with tags — failures are our prompt/repair bug.

BAD → dialect_script_impurity:
  English (or wrong-script) clinical body when Gujarati/Tamil/Meitei/Ol Chiki/Arabic/… is required.
GOOD:
  Full clinical prose in the assigned language AND exact script. Latin digits only inside ID tags
  (Aadhaar, phone, MRN, IFSC, email). Drug/lab names may stay Latin.

BAD → instruction_drift:
  Assigned domain is oncology but the note narrates generic TB / unrelated complaint.
GOOD:
  Chief complaint, findings, and plan clearly match the assigned clinical domain.

BAD → surrogate_plausibility_collapse:
  Persona district=Faridkot but VILLAGE/HOSPITAL in another state; ABHA local-part unrelated to PATIENT_NAME.
GOOD:
  All places grounded in persona state+district; names and ABHA coherent with PATIENT_NAME.

BAD → domain_persona_mismatch:
  Male persona with maternal delivery; age 70 with paediatric content;

GOOD:
  Sex/age in narrative and tags match persona exactly; maternal content is

BAD → missing tags / HOSPITAL_ID only:

BAD → entity stuffing / long SMS:
  AGE/PHONE/EMAIL repeated every chat turn; SMS that is a full discharge dump.

BAD → invented types / bad checksums:
  [[DATE|…]], [[POLICY_NUMBER|…]]; Aadhaar failing Verhoeff; toy phones like 1234567890.
GOOD:
  Only allow-listed TYPEs (billing: EMAIL→EMAIL_ADDRESS, ADDRESS→RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS;

  Fix ALL known issues together: correct ASCII tags + correct domain/persona +

payload.user_prompt:

CRITICAL: TYPE names are NEVER translated or localized — always exact ASCII allow-list (PATIENT_NAME, MRN, …). Forbidden: [[রোগীর_নাম|…]], [[रोगी_नाम|…]], [[PatientName|…]].
ID-like tagged values stay Latin/digits. Drug names / labs stay untagged.
DOMAIN ANCHOR: chief complaint, findings, and plan MUST match Tuberculosis & Non-Communicable Diseases — do not default to unrelated TB/generic content unless that is the assigned domain.
GEO ANCHOR: any DISTRICT/VILLAGE/HOSPITAL_NAME must be plausible for persona state=Uttar Pradesh, district=Kanpur Nagar.
NOTE: translation stage (not this draft) will put the body into Devanagari for Sanskrit.

LENGTH HARD LIMIT: entire SMS ≤ ~500 characters, but you MUST still include every mandatory entity tag below at least once (compact tags OK).

Persona anchors (must remain consistent; patient = this persona):
- sex / gender: Male
- age: 19
- marital_status: Never Married
- occupation: No Occupation / Retired / Homemaker
- education_level: Matric/Secondary
- zone: Urban
- state: Uttar Pradesh
- district: Kanpur Nagar
- first_language: Sanskrit
- clinical_persona_summary: A 19‑year‑old male from Kanpur Nagar, Uttar Pradesh, with a matriculation education, currently not employed. He resides in an urban setting and speaks Sanskrit as his first language, with functional English proficiency. His lifestyle includes occasional anxiety, yoga practice, and a preference for street food, while he manages academic pressures and deadlines.

Document format guide for Automated SMS Notifications (English structure; a later stage translates to the target language):
CRITICAL: keep the WHOLE SMS under ~400 characters / 2–4 sentences. Not a form dump.
MUST tag [[APPOINTMENT_ID|…]] whenever an appointment is mentioned.
Example:
[[PATIENT_NAME|Meena Devi]]: appt [[APPOINTMENT_ID|APT-240521-01]] at [[HOSPITAL_NAME|PHC Kanpur]] on 21-May 10:30 with [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Sharma]]. Confirm on [[PHONE_NUMBER|9876512340]]. MRN [[MRN|MRN-2024-001]].

MANDATORY inline entities (6 types) — EACH must appear ≥ once as [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]. Missing ANY is a hard failure:
- PATIENT_NAME — Patient Name e.g. [[PATIENT_NAME|Meena Devi]]
- PHONE_NUMBER — Phone Number e.g. [[PHONE_NUMBER|9876512340]]
- HOSPITAL_NAME — Hospital / Institution Name e.g. [[HOSPITAL_NAME|District Hospital Kanpur]]
- APPOINTMENT_ID — Appointment ID e.g. [[APPOINTMENT_ID|APT-240521-01]]
- DOCTOR_NAME — Doctor Name e.g. [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Anjali Sharma]]
- MRN — Medical Record Number (MRN) e.g. [[MRN|MRN-2024-0815-001]]

Before finishing, mentally checklist every mandatory TYPE above.
Do NOT invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, TOTAL_AMOUNT, GST_AMOUNT, GRAND_TOTAL). Put dates/times and money amounts in plain prose.
Produce ONLY the clinical document text with inline annotations. No markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.
