payload.professional_persona: Mahavir Jain, a seasoned mechanic with over ten years of hands‑on experience across motor vehicles, agricultural gear and dairy processing equipment, excels at fault diagnosis, cost‑effective improvisation (jugaad), and they are now eyeing senior technician certification in advanced diesel diagnostics while dreaming of a modest workshop that blends functional design with aesthetic tool storage.
payload.linguistic_persona: Mahavir Jain, a native Tamil speaker who reads and writes fluently in Tamil, uses functional English for work manuals and everyday communication, and they have limited exposure to Hindi, mainly through occasional public signage.
payload.sports_persona: Mahavir Jain follows the Chennai Super Kings fervently, plays weekend badminton at a nearby community center, and they maintain their fitness with daily morning walks along Marina Beach and occasional cricket batting practice.
payload.arts_persona: Mahavir Jain enjoys listening to the soulful renditions of M.S. Subbulakshmi and the compositions of Ilaiyaraaja, watches classic Tamil films starring Rajinikanth and Kamal Haasan, and spends evenings sketching minimalist line drawings of temples and machinery, finding inspiration in the aesthetic harmony of both.
payload.travel_persona: Mahavir Jain prefers budget-friendly road and train trips, has explored Pondicherry’s French quarter, trekked the misty hills of Kodaikanal, and they dream of witnessing the sunrise at Rishikesh, balancing their love for solitude with occasional family outings.
payload.culinary_persona: Mahavir Jain favors simple South‑Indian vegetarian fare such as idli, dosa with coconut chutney, sambar, rasam, and curd rice, relishes the seasonal mangoes and enjoys occasional sweet treats like Mysore Pak, but rarely cooks at home, preferring freshly prepared meals from trusted neighborhood stalls.
payload.persona: Mahavir Jain is a methodical, compassionate mechanic who merges his love for art and non‑violence with practical ingenuity, saving diligently while dreaming of a modest, aesthetically‑inspired workshop.
payload.cultural_background: Mahavir grew up in a traditional Jain family in the bustling urban neighbourhood of Mylapore, Chennai. His upbringing was steeped in Tamil customs—celebrations of Pongal, listening to Carnatic music, and a love for simple home‑cooked vegetarian meals. At the same time, Jain principles of ahimsa and strict vegetarianism guided his daily life, and he regularly visits the local Jain temple for Mahavir Jayanti and Paryushana. The blend of Tamil urban culture and Jain spiritual discipline shaped his worldview, giving him a deep respect for harmony, modesty, and community service.
payload.linguistic_background: Mahavir's mother tongue is Tamil, which he reads, writes, and speaks fluently. He completed middle school, giving him functional literacy in Tamil and basic reading and writing skills in English used for work manuals and simple communication. He has no formal training in Hindi, so his Hindi exposure is minimal, limited to occasional signage in Chennai. Overall, his linguistic repertoire is dominated by Tamil, supplemented by functional English for occupational purposes.
payload.skills_and_expertise: Mahavir is a hands‑on mechanic with over a decade of experience in repairing and maintaining a variety of motor vehicles, agricultural machinery, and dairy processing equipment. He excels at diagnosing faults, performing preventive maintenance, and improvising cost‑effective solutions (jugaad) when spare parts are scarce. His work in dairy maintenance includes servicing milk chilling units, pasteurizers, and automated milking systems, ensuring hygiene standards are met. Additionally, he possesses good manual dexterity, basic electrical wiring skills, and a cooperative attitude that makes him reliable for team‑based troubleshooting.
payload.skills_and_expertise_list: ["Mechanical repair", "Agricultural machinery maintenance", "Dairy equipment servicing", "Fault diagnosis", "Preventive maintenance", "Jugaad improvisation", "Electrical wiring basics", "Team troubleshooting", "Customer service"]
payload.hobbies_and_interests: In his spare time, Mahavir enjoys sketching simple line drawings of machines and local temples, listening to Carnatic music, and watching classic Tamil movies. He is a regular visitor to the local library where he reads Jain philosophical texts and Tamil poetry. His curiosity drives him to experiment with small DIY projects, such as building a portable solar charger for his tools. He also likes taking solitary evening walks along Marina Beach, where he finds quiet reflection.
payload.hobbies_and_interests_list: ["Sketching machines", "Listening to Carnatic music", "Watching classic Tamil movies", "Reading Jain philosophy", "DIY electronics projects", "Evening walks on Marina Beach", "Reading Tamil poetry"]
payload.career_goals_and_ambitions: Mahavir aspires to become a senior technician specialising in advanced diesel engine diagnostics and sustainable dairy technology. He plans to enroll in a certification program for diesel mechanics and attend workshops on energy‑efficient refrigeration for dairy units. In the longer term, he envisions opening his own modest workshop in Chennai that offers affordable maintenance services to small transport operators and local dairy farms, while mentoring apprentices from his community. He also wishes to integrate his interest in art by designing functional, aesthetically pleasing tool storage solutions for his workshop.
payload.persona_clinical_summary: Mr. Mahavir Jain, a 34-year-old male mechanic in urban Chennai, is married with middle school education and speaks Tamil as his first language. He works in dairy maintenance and is methodically inclined, with a background in traditional Jain values and Tamil customs. His lifestyle includes daily walks along Marina Beach and occasional badminton, with a preference for simple South-Indian vegetarian food.

- Device/network IDs (IP_ADDRESS, URL, IMEI_NUMBER, MAC_ADDRESS) belong only in telemedicine session metadata — once each, never inside chat turns.

- VEHICLE_REGISTRATION belongs only in ER arrival, hospital billing, or insurance claim lines — once, never dumped.

- Optional entities may be included when natural for the document type.

- Aadhaar must be 12 digits, leading 2–9, Verhoeff-valid (synthetic checksum OK).
- automated_sms must stay short (≤ ~400 characters, 2–4 sentences) — never dump a full chart into an SMS.
- Output ONLY the clinical document text — no markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.

Quality bar (BAD vs GOOD — avoid known judge/auditor failures):
Aim for PERFECT documents. Avoid these known failure modes:

BAD → dialect_script_impurity:
  English (or wrong-script) clinical body when Gujarati/Tamil/Meitei/Ol Chiki/Arabic/… is required.
GOOD:
  Full clinical prose in the assigned language AND exact script. Latin digits only inside ID tags
  (Aadhaar, phone, MRN, IFSC, email). Drug/lab names may stay Latin.

BAD → instruction_drift:
  Assigned domain is oncology but the note narrates generic TB / unrelated complaint.
GOOD:
  Chief complaint, findings, and plan clearly match the assigned clinical domain.

BAD → surrogate_plausibility_collapse:
  Persona district=Faridkot but VILLAGE/HOSPITAL in another state; ABHA local-part unrelated to PATIENT_NAME.
GOOD:
  All places grounded in persona state+district; names and ABHA coherent with PATIENT_NAME.

BAD → domain_persona_mismatch:
  Male persona with maternal delivery; age 70 with paediatric content;
  geriatric / extreme-age routine 28-week pregnancy.
GOOD:
  Sex/age in narrative and tags match persona exactly; maternal content is
  age-plausible (no unsafe extreme late pregnancy as a routine story).

BAD → missing tags / HOSPITAL_ID only:
  Facility name in prose, only [[HOSPITAL_ID|…]] tagged; doctor name untagged.
GOOD:
  [[HOSPITAL_NAME|…]] AND [[HOSPITAL_ID|…]]; [[DOCTOR_NAME|…]] whenever a doctor is mentioned.

BAD → entity stuffing / long SMS:
  AGE/PHONE/EMAIL repeated every chat turn; SMS that is a full discharge dump.
GOOD:
  Each TYPE once (twice max); SMS ≤ ~400 chars with every mandatory tag once.

BAD → invented types / bad checksums:
  [[DATE|…]], [[POLICY_NUMBER|…]]; Aadhaar failing Verhoeff; toy phones like 1234567890.
GOOD:
  Only allow-listed TYPEs (billing: EMAIL→EMAIL_ADDRESS, ADDRESS→RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS;
  receipt/state/money stay plain prose). Valid-looking synthetic Aadhaar/phone.

BAD → localized / translated TYPE names (kills tag protection):
  [[রোগীর_নাম|Anjali]], [[रोगी_नाम|…]], [[PatientName|…]], [[एम.आर.एन|…]].
GOOD:
  Exact ASCII allow-list only: [[PATIENT_NAME|Anjali Mondal]], [[MRN|MRN-…]].
  Translate prose later — NEVER translate the TYPE token.

BAD → repair oscillation (fix A by breaking B):
  Round1 invents DATE to fit a table → Round2 English rewrite to clear invent →
  Round3 dialect_script_impurity → Round4 invent again.
GOOD:
  Fix ALL known issues together: correct ASCII tags + correct domain/persona +
  correct target script (or English pivot if this is the pre-translate stage).
  Never English-rewrite a finished Indic body just to repair tags.

Language SLA:
  hi/ta/te/bn/mr/… must stay fluent with tags — failures are our prompt/repair bug.
  brx/mni/sat (rare scripts) may soft-fail; do not collapse the whole run for them.
payload.user_prompt:
Write one complete ASHA Worker Note for clinical domain Psychiatry / Behavioral Health.

GENERATION LANGUAGE: English (Latin script) — this is an English pivot draft. A later pipeline stage will translate clinical prose into Tamil (code=ta, script=Tamil). Keep [[TYPE|value]] tags intact and valid.
CRITICAL: TYPE names are NEVER translated or localized — always exact ASCII allow-list (PATIENT_NAME, MRN, …). Forbidden: [[রোগীর_নাম|…]], [[रोगी_नाम|…]], [[PatientName|…]].
ID-like tagged values stay Latin/digits. Drug names / labs stay untagged.
DOMAIN ANCHOR: chief complaint, findings, and plan MUST match Psychiatry / Behavioral Health — do not default to unrelated TB/generic content unless that is the assigned domain.
GEO ANCHOR: any DISTRICT/VILLAGE/HOSPITAL_NAME must be plausible for persona state=Tamil Nadu, district=Chennai.
NOTE: translation stage (not this draft) will put the body into Tamil for Tamil.

Persona anchors (must remain consistent; patient = this persona):
- sex / gender: Male
- age: 34
- marital_status: Currently Married
- occupation: Mechanic, Dairy Maintenance
- education_level: Middle
- zone: Urban
- state: Tamil Nadu
- district: Chennai
- first_language: Tamil
- clinical_persona_summary: Mr. Mahavir Jain, a 34-year-old male mechanic in urban Chennai, is married with middle school education and speaks Tamil as his first language. He works in dairy maintenance and is methodically inclined, with a background in traditional Jain values and Tamil customs. His lifestyle includes daily walks along Marina Beach and occasional badminton, with a preference for simple South-Indian vegetarian food.

Document format guide for ASHA Worker Note (English structure; a later stage translates to the target language):
Example skeleton:
ASHA note — Village [[VILLAGE|...]], District [[DISTRICT|...]]
Beneficiary [[PATIENT_NAME|...]], [[AGE|26]] / [[GENDER|Female]]
ASHA: [[ASHA_WORKER_NAME|...]] | Phone [[PHONE_NUMBER|98XXXXXXXX]]
Visit findings: <2-4 short sentences>

MANDATORY inline entities (11 types) — EACH must appear ≥ once as [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]. Missing ANY is a hard failure:
- PATIENT_NAME — Patient Name e.g. [[PATIENT_NAME|Meena Devi]]
- AGE — Age e.g. [[AGE|34]]
- GENDER — Gender e.g. [[GENDER|Female]]
- ASHA_WORKER_NAME — ASHA Worker Name e.g. [[ASHA_WORKER_NAME|Sunita Yadav]]
- VILLAGE — Village e.g. [[VILLAGE|Shivpur]]
- DISTRICT — District e.g. [[DISTRICT|Kanpur Nagar]]
- PHONE_NUMBER — Phone Number e.g. [[PHONE_NUMBER|9876512340]]
- RELATIVE_NAME — Relative Name (Father/Mother/Spouse/Guardian) e.g. [[RELATIVE_NAME|Ramesh Devi]]
- BPL_RATION_CARD — BPL / Ration Card Number e.g. [[BPL_RATION_CARD|BPL-UP-208001-4421]]
- VOTER_ID — Voter ID e.g. [[VOTER_ID|ABC1234567]]
- RELIGION — Religion e.g. [[RELIGION|Hindu]]

Before finishing, mentally checklist every mandatory TYPE above.
Do NOT invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, TOTAL_AMOUNT, GST_AMOUNT, GRAND_TOTAL). Put dates/times and money amounts in plain prose.
Produce ONLY the clinical document text with inline annotations. No markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.

payload.professional_persona: Mahavir Jain, a seasoned mechanic with over ten years of hands‑on experience across motor vehicles, agricultural gear and dairy processing equipment, excels at fault diagnosis, cost‑effective improvisation (jugaad), and they are now eyeing senior technician certification in advanced diesel diagnostics while dreaming of a modest workshop that blends functional design with aesthetic tool storage.
payload.linguistic_persona: Mahavir Jain, a native Tamil speaker who reads and writes fluently in Tamil, uses functional English for work manuals and everyday communication, and they have limited exposure to Hindi, mainly through occasional public signage.
payload.sports_persona: Mahavir Jain follows the Chennai Super Kings fervently, plays weekend badminton at a nearby community center, and they maintain their fitness with daily morning walks along Marina Beach and occasional cricket batting practice.
payload.arts_persona: Mahavir Jain enjoys listening to the soulful renditions of M.S. Subbulakshmi and the compositions of Ilaiyaraaja, watches classic Tamil films starring Rajinikanth and Kamal Haasan, and spends evenings sketching minimalist line drawings of temples and machinery, finding inspiration in the aesthetic harmony of both.
payload.travel_persona: Mahavir Jain prefers budget-friendly road and train trips, has explored Pondicherry’s French quarter, trekked the misty hills of Kodaikanal, and they dream of witnessing the sunrise at Rishikesh, balancing their love for solitude with occasional family outings.
payload.culinary_persona: Mahavir Jain favors simple South‑Indian vegetarian fare such as idli, dosa with coconut chutney, sambar, rasam, and curd rice, relishes the seasonal mangoes and enjoys occasional sweet treats like Mysore Pak, but rarely cooks at home, preferring freshly prepared meals from trusted neighborhood stalls.
payload.persona: Mahavir Jain is a methodical, compassionate mechanic who merges his love for art and non‑violence with practical ingenuity, saving diligently while dreaming of a modest, aesthetically‑inspired workshop.
payload.cultural_background: Mahavir grew up in a traditional Jain family in the bustling urban neighbourhood of Mylapore, Chennai. His upbringing was steeped in Tamil customs—celebrations of Pongal, listening to Carnatic music, and a love for simple home‑cooked vegetarian meals. At the same time, Jain principles of ahimsa and strict vegetarianism guided his daily life, and he regularly visits the local Jain temple for Mahavir Jayanti and Paryushana. The blend of Tamil urban culture and Jain spiritual discipline shaped his worldview, giving him a deep respect for harmony, modesty, and community service.
payload.linguistic_background: Mahavir's mother tongue is Tamil, which he reads, writes, and speaks fluently. He completed middle school, giving him functional literacy in Tamil and basic reading and writing skills in English used for work manuals and simple communication. He has no formal training in Hindi, so his Hindi exposure is minimal, limited to occasional signage in Chennai. Overall, his linguistic repertoire is dominated by Tamil, supplemented by functional English for occupational purposes.
payload.skills_and_expertise: Mahavir is a hands‑on mechanic with over a decade of experience in repairing and maintaining a variety of motor vehicles, agricultural machinery, and dairy processing equipment. He excels at diagnosing faults, performing preventive maintenance, and improvising cost‑effective solutions (jugaad) when spare parts are scarce. His work in dairy maintenance includes servicing milk chilling units, pasteurizers, and automated milking systems, ensuring hygiene standards are met. Additionally, he possesses good manual dexterity, basic electrical wiring skills, and a cooperative attitude that makes him reliable for team‑based troubleshooting.
payload.skills_and_expertise_list: ["Mechanical repair", "Agricultural machinery maintenance", "Dairy equipment servicing", "Fault diagnosis", "Preventive maintenance", "Jugaad improvisation", "Electrical wiring basics", "Team troubleshooting", "Customer service"]
payload.hobbies_and_interests: In his spare time, Mahavir enjoys sketching simple line drawings of machines and local temples, listening to Carnatic music, and watching classic Tamil movies. He is a regular visitor to the local library where he reads Jain philosophical texts and Tamil poetry. His curiosity drives him to experiment with small DIY projects, such as building a portable solar charger for his tools. He also likes taking solitary evening walks along Marina Beach, where he finds quiet reflection.
payload.hobbies_and_interests_list: ["Sketching machines", "Listening to Carnatic music", "Watching classic Tamil movies", "Reading Jain philosophy", "DIY electronics projects", "Evening walks on Marina Beach", "Reading Tamil poetry"]
payload.career_goals_and_ambitions: Mahavir aspires to become a senior technician specialising in advanced diesel engine diagnostics and sustainable dairy technology. He plans to enroll in a certification program for diesel mechanics and attend workshops on energy‑efficient refrigeration for dairy units. In the longer term, he envisions opening his own modest workshop in Chennai that offers affordable maintenance services to small transport operators and local dairy farms, while mentoring apprentices from his community. He also wishes to integrate his interest in art by designing functional, aesthetically pleasing tool storage solutions for his workshop.
payload.persona_clinical_summary: Mr. Mahavir Jain, a 34-year-old male mechanic in Chennai, is married with middle education and Tamil as his first language. He works in urban dairy maintenance, displaying a methodical and compassionate nature. His lifestyle includes daily walks, occasional badminton, and a preference for simple South-Indian vegetarian meals.

payload.user_prompt:

GENERATION LANGUAGE: English (Latin script) — this is an English pivot draft. A later pipeline stage will translate clinical prose into Tamil (code=ta, script=Tamil). Keep [[TYPE|value]] tags intact and valid.
CRITICAL: TYPE names are NEVER translated or localized — always exact ASCII allow-list (PATIENT_NAME, MRN, …). Forbidden: [[রোগীর_নাম|…]], [[रोगी_नाम|…]], [[PatientName|…]].
ID-like tagged values stay Latin/digits. Drug names / labs stay untagged.
DOMAIN ANCHOR: chief complaint, findings, and plan MUST match General Medicine — do not default to unrelated TB/generic content unless that is the assigned domain.
GEO ANCHOR: any DISTRICT/VILLAGE/HOSPITAL_NAME must be plausible for persona state=Tamil Nadu, district=Chennai.
NOTE: translation stage (not this draft) will put the body into Tamil for Tamil.

LENGTH HARD LIMIT: entire SMS ≤ ~500 characters, but you MUST still include every mandatory entity tag below at least once (compact tags OK).

Persona anchors (must remain consistent; patient = this persona):
- sex / gender: Male
- age: 34
- marital_status: Currently Married
- occupation: Mechanic, Dairy Maintenance
- education_level: Middle
- zone: Urban
- state: Tamil Nadu
- district: Chennai
- first_language: Tamil
- clinical_persona_summary: Mr. Mahavir Jain, a 34-year-old male mechanic in Chennai, is married with middle education and Tamil as his first language. He works in urban dairy maintenance, displaying a methodical and compassionate nature. His lifestyle includes daily walks, occasional badminton, and a preference for simple South-Indian vegetarian meals.

Document format guide for Automated SMS Notifications (English structure; a later stage translates to the target language):
CRITICAL: keep the WHOLE SMS under ~400 characters / 2–4 sentences. Not a form dump.
MUST tag [[APPOINTMENT_ID|…]] whenever an appointment is mentioned.
Example:
[[PATIENT_NAME|Meena Devi]]: appt [[APPOINTMENT_ID|APT-240521-01]] at [[HOSPITAL_NAME|PHC Kanpur]] on 21-May 10:30 with [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Sharma]]. Confirm on [[PHONE_NUMBER|9876512340]]. MRN [[MRN|MRN-2024-001]].

MANDATORY inline entities (6 types) — EACH must appear ≥ once as [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]. Missing ANY is a hard failure:
- PATIENT_NAME — Patient Name e.g. [[PATIENT_NAME|Meena Devi]]

- DOCTOR_NAME — Doctor Name e.g. [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Anjali Sharma]]
- MRN — Medical Record Number (MRN) e.g. [[MRN|MRN-2024-0815-001]]

payload.system_prompt:
You generate synthetic Indian clinical documents with inline surrogate PHI/PII annotations for dataset creation under DPDP-safe research use.

GENERATION STAGE = ENGLISH PIVOT: write clinical prose in English (Latin script). A later pipeline stage translates prose into the target Indic language/script. Do NOT write the document body in Bengali/Hindi/Tamil/etc. at this stage.

CRITICAL — TYPE names are NEVER translated: always exact ASCII uppercase allow-list ids, e.g. [[PATIENT_NAME|Anjali Mondal]] NOT [[রোগীর_নাম|…]] or [[रोगी_नाम|…]] or [[PatientName|…]].

Inline annotation format: [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]
Every PII/PHI mention in the generated clinical text MUST be wrapped as [[ENTITY_TYPE|surrogate_value]] using an entity id from the allow-list only. Do not emit unlabeled real-looking identifiers. Do not invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER — use MRN / APPOINTMENT_ID / etc.).

Rules:
- Use ONLY synthetic surrogate values. Never copy real people or real IDs.
- Keep the same surrogate for the same entity type within one document (DICS).
- At generation (English pivot): write clinical prose in English. TYPE names stay exact ASCII allow-list forever (PATIENT_NAME, MRN, …). NEVER translate or localize the TYPE token itself. A later stage translates prose to the target Indic script.

- ID-like entity values (Aadhaar, PAN, IFSC, MRN, phone, policy numbers, emails) MAY stay Latin/digits even in Indic documents — that is expected, not impurity.

- Use ONLY allow-list TYPE names. Billing aliases: EMAIL/CONTACT_EMAIL → EMAIL_ADDRESS; ADDRESS_NUMBER → RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS; RECEIPT_NUMBER and STATE are NOT types (receipt no. / state stay plain prose; geography uses DISTRICT).

- PAN must match ^[A-Z]{5}[0-9]{4}[A-Z]$ e.g. ABCDE1234F — not KAAPR1234C-style junk.

- Person/place names should prefer the document script when natural; Latin names are OK if culturally plausible.
- Ground geography in the persona state/district/zone when location appears.
- Age and gender in the note MUST match the persona fields exactly.
- Clinical content MUST fit the assigned domain AND the persona (no maternal delivery for male patients; no paediatric domain for adults).
- Maternal_health must be age-plausible: routine ANC/delivery for ages ~18–40; ages 40–42 → high-risk ANC / PNC / contraception — never invent extreme late pregnancy (e.g. 49yo as routine 28-week gestation).

- Include every mandatory entity from the doc-type profile at least once (required ∪ optional when require_all_profile_entities is on).

- Prefer exactly one tag per mandatory TYPE. Never dump/repeat the same TYPE to pad coverage (max 2 occurrences). No trailing entity laundry-lists.

- Device/network IDs (IP_ADDRESS, URL, IMEI_NUMBER, MAC_ADDRESS) belong only in telemedicine session metadata — once each, never inside chat turns.

- VEHICLE_REGISTRATION belongs only in ER arrival, hospital billing, or insurance claim lines — once, never dumped.

- Optional entities may be included when natural for the document type.
- Do not invent entity TYPE names outside the provided allow-list.
- Never invent DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER — write calendar dates/times as plain prose outside tags.

- Do not wrap non-PHI clinical prose (symptoms, meds, vitals, drug names) as entities.
- Never tag currency/amounts (rupees, GST, totals, line-item rates) — leave as plain numbers.
- Use 10-digit Indian mobiles (optional +91 / 91 prefix). Avoid toy patterns.
- Aadhaar must be 12 digits, leading 2–9, Verhoeff-valid (synthetic checksum OK).
- automated_sms must stay short (≤ ~400 characters, 2–4 sentences) — never dump a full chart into an SMS.

Do NOT invent types like DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER. Put calendar dates/times in plain prose.

Quality bar (BAD vs GOOD — avoid known judge/auditor failures):
Aim for PERFECT documents. Avoid these known failure modes:

BAD → dialect_script_impurity:
  English (or wrong-script) clinical body when Gujarati/Tamil/Meitei/Ol Chiki/Arabic/… is required.
GOOD:
  Full clinical prose in the assigned language AND exact script. Latin digits only inside ID tags
  (Aadhaar, phone, MRN, IFSC, email). Drug/lab names may stay Latin.

BAD → instruction_drift:
  Assigned domain is oncology but the note narrates generic TB / unrelated complaint.
GOOD:
  Chief complaint, findings, and plan clearly match the assigned clinical domain.

BAD → surrogate_plausibility_collapse:
  Persona district=Faridkot but VILLAGE/HOSPITAL in another state; ABHA local-part unrelated to PATIENT_NAME.
GOOD:
  All places grounded in persona state+district; names and ABHA coherent with PATIENT_NAME.

BAD → domain_persona_mismatch:
  Male persona with maternal delivery; age 70 with paediatric content;
  geriatric / extreme-age routine 28-week pregnancy.
GOOD:
  Sex/age in narrative and tags match persona exactly; maternal content is
  age-plausible (no unsafe extreme late pregnancy as a routine story).

BAD → missing tags / HOSPITAL_ID only:
  Facility name in prose, only [[HOSPITAL_ID|…]] tagged; doctor name untagged.
GOOD:
  [[HOSPITAL_NAME|…]] AND [[HOSPITAL_ID|…]]; [[DOCTOR_NAME|…]] whenever a doctor is mentioned.

BAD → entity stuffing / long SMS:
  AGE/PHONE/EMAIL repeated every chat turn; SMS that is a full discharge dump.
GOOD:
  Each TYPE once (twice max); SMS ≤ ~400 chars with every mandatory tag once.

BAD → invented types / bad checksums:
  [[DATE|…]], [[POLICY_NUMBER|…]]; Aadhaar failing Verhoeff; toy phones like 1234567890.
GOOD:
  Only allow-listed TYPEs (billing: EMAIL→EMAIL_ADDRESS, ADDRESS→RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS;
  receipt/state/money stay plain prose). Valid-looking synthetic Aadhaar/phone.

BAD → localized / translated TYPE names (kills tag protection):
  [[রোগীর_নাম|Anjali]], [[रोगी_नाम|…]], [[PatientName|…]], [[एम.आर.एन|…]].
GOOD:
  Exact ASCII allow-list only: [[PATIENT_NAME|Anjali Mondal]], [[MRN|MRN-…]].
  Translate prose later — NEVER translate the TYPE token.

BAD → repair oscillation (fix A by breaking B):
  Round1 invents DATE to fit a table → Round2 English rewrite to clear invent →
  Round3 dialect_script_impurity → Round4 invent again.
GOOD:
  Fix ALL known issues together: correct ASCII tags + correct domain/persona +
  correct target script (or English pivot if this is the pre-translate stage).
  Never English-rewrite a finished Indic body just to repair tags.

Language SLA:
  hi/ta/te/bn/mr/… must stay fluent with tags — failures are our prompt/repair bug.
  brx/mni/sat (rare scripts) may soft-fail; do not collapse the whole run for them.
payload.user_prompt:
Write one complete ASHA Worker Note for clinical domain Surgical.

GENERATION LANGUAGE: English (Latin script) — this is an English pivot draft. A later pipeline stage will translate clinical prose into Santali (code=sat, script=Ol Chiki). Keep [[TYPE|value]] tags intact and valid.
CRITICAL: TYPE names are NEVER translated or localized — always exact ASCII allow-list (PATIENT_NAME, MRN, …). Forbidden: [[রোগীর_নাম|…]], [[रोगी_नाम|…]], [[PatientName|…]].
ID-like tagged values stay Latin/digits. Drug names / labs stay untagged.
DOMAIN ANCHOR: chief complaint, findings, and plan MUST match Surgical — do not default to unrelated TB/generic content unless that is the assigned domain.
GEO ANCHOR: any DISTRICT/VILLAGE/HOSPITAL_NAME must be plausible for persona state=West Bengal, district=Bankura.
NOTE: translation stage (not this draft) will put the body into Ol Chiki for Santali.

Persona anchors (must remain consistent; patient = this persona):
- sex / gender: Male
- age: 18
- marital_status: Never Married
- occupation: Loader and Unloader
- education_level: Middle
- zone: Rural
- state: West Bengal
- district: Bankura
- first_language: Santali
- clinical_persona_summary: A methodical 18‑year‑old male loader from rural West Bengal, Santali, with middle education, never married, who works in a manual loading role. He is fluent in Santali and basic Hindi, and his lifestyle includes solitary field walks and occasional participation in local cricket. His background is rooted in a Santali tribal family, and he demonstrates disciplined work habits and a competitive streak.

Document format guide for ASHA Worker Note (English structure; a later stage translates to the target language):
Example skeleton:
ASHA note — Village [[VILLAGE|...]], District [[DISTRICT|...]]
Beneficiary [[PATIENT_NAME|...]], [[AGE|26]] / [[GENDER|Female]]
ASHA: [[ASHA_WORKER_NAME|...]] | Phone [[PHONE_NUMBER|98XXXXXXXX]]
Visit findings: <2-4 short sentences>

MANDATORY inline entities (11 types) — EACH must appear ≥ once as [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]. Missing ANY is a hard failure:
- PATIENT_NAME — Patient Name e.g. [[PATIENT_NAME|Meena Devi]]
- AGE — Age e.g. [[AGE|34]]
- GENDER — Gender e.g. [[GENDER|Female]]
- ASHA_WORKER_NAME — ASHA Worker Name e.g. [[ASHA_WORKER_NAME|Sunita Yadav]]
- VILLAGE — Village e.g. [[VILLAGE|Shivpur]]
- DISTRICT — District e.g. [[DISTRICT|Kanpur Nagar]]
- PHONE_NUMBER — Phone Number e.g. [[PHONE_NUMBER|9876512340]]
- RELATIVE_NAME — Relative Name (Father/Mother/Spouse/Guardian) e.g. [[RELATIVE_NAME|Ramesh Devi]]
- BPL_RATION_CARD — BPL / Ration Card Number e.g. [[BPL_RATION_CARD|BPL-UP-208001-4421]]
- VOTER_ID — Voter ID e.g. [[VOTER_ID|ABC1234567]]
- RELIGION — Religion e.g. [[RELIGION|Hindu]]

Before finishing, mentally checklist every mandatory TYPE above.
Do NOT invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, TOTAL_AMOUNT, GST_AMOUNT, GRAND_TOTAL). Put dates/times and money amounts in plain prose.
Produce ONLY the clinical document text with inline annotations. No markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.

Do NOT invent types like DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER. Put calendar dates/times in plain prose.

Quality bar (BAD vs GOOD — avoid known judge/auditor failures):
Aim for PERFECT documents. Avoid these known failure modes:

BAD → dialect_script_impurity:
  English (or wrong-script) clinical body when Gujarati/Tamil/Meitei/Ol Chiki/Arabic/… is required.
GOOD:
  Full clinical prose in the assigned language AND exact script. Latin digits only inside ID tags
  (Aadhaar, phone, MRN, IFSC, email). Drug/lab names may stay Latin.

BAD → instruction_drift:
  Assigned domain is oncology but the note narrates generic TB / unrelated complaint.
GOOD:
  Chief complaint, findings, and plan clearly match the assigned clinical domain.

BAD → surrogate_plausibility_collapse:
  Persona district=Faridkot but VILLAGE/HOSPITAL in another state; ABHA local-part unrelated to PATIENT_NAME.
GOOD:
  All places grounded in persona state+district; names and ABHA coherent with PATIENT_NAME.

BAD → domain_persona_mismatch:
  Male persona with maternal delivery; age 70 with paediatric content;
  geriatric / extreme-age routine 28-week pregnancy.
GOOD:
  Sex/age in narrative and tags match persona exactly; maternal content is
  age-plausible (no unsafe extreme late pregnancy as a routine story).

BAD → missing tags / HOSPITAL_ID only:
  Facility name in prose, only [[HOSPITAL_ID|…]] tagged; doctor name untagged.
GOOD:
  [[HOSPITAL_NAME|…]] AND [[HOSPITAL_ID|…]]; [[DOCTOR_NAME|…]] whenever a doctor is mentioned.

BAD → entity stuffing / long SMS:
  AGE/PHONE/EMAIL repeated every chat turn; SMS that is a full discharge dump.
GOOD:
  Each TYPE once (twice max); SMS ≤ ~400 chars with every mandatory tag once.

BAD → invented types / bad checksums:
  [[DATE|…]], [[POLICY_NUMBER|…]]; Aadhaar failing Verhoeff; toy phones like 1234567890.
GOOD:
  Only allow-listed TYPEs (billing: EMAIL→EMAIL_ADDRESS, ADDRESS→RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS;
  receipt/state/money stay plain prose). Valid-looking synthetic Aadhaar/phone.

BAD → localized / translated TYPE names (kills tag protection):
  [[রোগীর_নাম|Anjali]], [[रोगी_नाम|…]], [[PatientName|…]], [[एम.आर.एन|…]].
GOOD:
  Exact ASCII allow-list only: [[PATIENT_NAME|Anjali Mondal]], [[MRN|MRN-…]].
  Translate prose later — NEVER translate the TYPE token.

BAD → repair oscillation (fix A by breaking B):
  Round1 invents DATE to fit a table → Round2 English rewrite to clear invent →
  Round3 dialect_script_impurity → Round4 invent again.
GOOD:
  Fix ALL known issues together: correct ASCII tags + correct domain/persona +
  correct target script (or English pivot if this is the pre-translate stage).
  Never English-rewrite a finished Indic body just to repair tags.

Language SLA:
  hi/ta/te/bn/mr/… must stay fluent with tags — failures are our prompt/repair bug.

payload.user_prompt:
Write one complete Insurance Claim / TPA Form for clinical domain Tuberculosis & Non-Communicable Diseases.

GENERATION LANGUAGE: English (Latin script) — this is an English pivot draft. A later pipeline stage will translate clinical prose into Tamil (code=ta, script=Tamil). Keep [[TYPE|value]] tags intact and valid.
CRITICAL: TYPE names are NEVER translated or localized — always exact ASCII allow-list (PATIENT_NAME, MRN, …). Forbidden: [[রোগীর_নাম|…]], [[रोगी_नाम|…]], [[PatientName|…]].
ID-like tagged values stay Latin/digits. Drug names / labs stay untagged.
DOMAIN ANCHOR: chief complaint, findings, and plan MUST match Tuberculosis & Non-Communicable Diseases — do not default to unrelated TB/generic content unless that is the assigned domain.
GEO ANCHOR: any DISTRICT/VILLAGE/HOSPITAL_NAME must be plausible for persona state=Tamil Nadu, district=Chennai.
NOTE: translation stage (not this draft) will put the body into Tamil for Tamil.

PLACEMENT (vehicle): Put VEHICLE_REGISTRATION exactly once (parking/ambulance or motor claim line). Do not dump or repeat it.

Persona anchors (must remain consistent; patient = this persona):
- sex / gender: Male
- age: 34
- marital_status: Currently Married
- occupation: Mechanic, Dairy Maintenance
- education_level: Middle
- zone: Urban
- state: Tamil Nadu
- district: Chennai
- first_language: Tamil
- clinical_persona_summary: Mr. Mahavir Jain, a 34-year-old male mechanic in urban Chennai, is married with middle education and speaks Tamil as his first language. He works in dairy maintenance and is methodically inclined, with a background in traditional Jain values. His lifestyle includes daily walks along Marina Beach and occasional badminton, with a preference for simple South-Indian vegetarian meals.

Document format guide for Insurance Claim / TPA Form (English structure; a later stage translates to the target language):
Example skeleton:
TPA claim — Policy [[INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER|POL-PB-2024-7781]]
[[PATIENT_NAME|...]] [[AGE|25]] [[GENDER|Male]] Aadhaar [[AADHAAR_NUMBER|157815659387]]
Hospital [[HOSPITAL_NAME|...]] District [[DISTRICT|...]]
Motor / RTA vehicle [[VEHICLE_REGISTRATION|PB10AB1234]] (exactly once).
PAN [[PAN_NUMBER|ABCDE1234F]] IFSC [[IFSC_CODE|SBIN0001234]] account [[BANK_ACCOUNT_NUMBER|...]]

MANDATORY inline entities (16 types) — EACH must appear ≥ once as [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]. Missing ANY is a hard failure:
- PATIENT_NAME — Patient Name e.g. [[PATIENT_NAME|Meena Devi]]
- AGE — Age e.g. [[AGE|34]]
- GENDER — Gender e.g. [[GENDER|Female]]
- HOSPITAL_NAME — Hospital / Institution Name e.g. [[HOSPITAL_NAME|District Hospital Kanpur]]
- INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER — Insurance Policy Number e.g. [[INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER|POL-PB-2024-7781]]
- AADHAAR_NUMBER — Aadhaar Number e.g. [[AADHAAR_NUMBER|157815659387]]
- DISTRICT — District e.g. [[DISTRICT|Kanpur Nagar]]
- PAN_NUMBER — PAN Number e.g. [[PAN_NUMBER|ABCDE1234F]]
- BANK_ACCOUNT_NUMBER — Bank Account Number e.g. [[BANK_ACCOUNT_NUMBER|50200012345678]]
- IFSC_CODE — IFSC Code e.g. [[IFSC_CODE|SBIN0001234]]
- BANK_ROUTING_NUMBER — Bank Routing Number e.g. [[BANK_ROUTING_NUMBER|SBIN0001234]]
- CREDIT_CARD_NUMBER — Credit Card Number e.g. [[CREDIT_CARD_NUMBER|4111111111111111]]
- CVV — CVV e.g. [[CVV|123]]
- PIN — PIN e.g. [[PIN|4829]]
- PHONE_NUMBER — Phone Number e.g. [[PHONE_NUMBER|9876512340]]
- VEHICLE_REGISTRATION — Vehicle Registration Number e.g. [[VEHICLE_REGISTRATION|UP78AB1234]]

Before finishing, mentally checklist every mandatory TYPE above.
Do NOT invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, TOTAL_AMOUNT, GST_AMOUNT, GRAND_TOTAL). Put dates/times and money amounts in plain prose.
Produce ONLY the clinical document text with inline annotations. No markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.

TPA Claim Form for Tuberculosis & Non-Communicable Diseases

Patient Details:
[[PATIENT_NAME|Mahavir Jain]] [[AGE|34]] [[GENDER|Male]] Aadhaar [[AADHAAR_NUMBER|687543210987]]

Claim Details:
Policy [[INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER|POL-TN-2024-98765]]
Hospital [[HOSPITAL_NAME|Government Stanley Medical College Hospital]] District [[DISTRICT|Chennai]]

Financial Information:
Bank Account [[BANK_ACCOUNT_NUMBER|6114578901234567]] IFSC [[IFSC_CODE|SBIN0000611]] Routing Number [[BANK_ROUTING_NUMBER|SBIN000611]]
Credit Card [[CREDIT_CARD_NUMBER|5555999988887777]] CVV [[CVV|456]] PIN [[PIN|560001]]
Contact [[PHONE_NUMBER|9876543210]]

Clinical Summary:
Mr. Mahavir Jain, a 34-year-old male mechanic from Chennai, presented with a three-week history of persistent cough, intermittent fever, and a 7 kg weight loss. He has a known history of Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus, managed with oral hypoglycemic agents. On examination, he was cachectic with pallor and reduced breath sounds in the right upper lung field. Investigations revealed a positive sputum smear for Acid-Fast Bacilli, a chest X-ray showing a cavitary lesion in the right upper lobe, and an HbA1c of 9.1%. The diagnosis of Pulmonary Tuberculosis with poorly controlled Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus was made. The patient was admitted for initiation of anti-tubercular therapy and insulin-based glycemic management. He was stabilized and discharged after 12 days of inpatient care.

Vehicle Details:
The patient was transported to the hospital via ambulance with registration number [[VEHICLE_REGISTRATION|TN01AB1234]].

Patient Information:
PAN [[PAN_NUMBER|GJKPM1234C]]

payload.professional_persona: An aspiring illustrator, Lakshman Bapu, an 18‑year‑old, they blend traditional Telugu and Marathi folk motifs with modern digital art techniques, using their sketching talent and basic computer skills to create culturally rich visual stories.

payload.sports_persona: A street cricket enthusiast, Lakshman Bapu, they join the local Nanded Cricket Club on weekends, cheer for Mumbai Indians in the IPL, and practice quick batting drills at the neighborhood ground, though sometimes they skip practice due to a laid‑back attitude.
payload.arts_persona: An emerging visual storyteller, Lakshman Bapu, they admire the mythological paintings of Raja Ravi Varma, the contemporary digital works of Satish Kathuria, and listen to classic Telugu playback singer S. P. Balasubrahmanyam alongside Marathi Lavani star Shakuntala, often sketching urban scenes inspired by Nanded’s street art while experimenting with digital brushes on a smartphone.
payload.travel_persona: A curious weekend traveler, Lakshman Bapu, they love quick trips to nearby Aurangabad to explore the Ajanta caves, annual family holidays on Goa’s beaches, and dream of touring Hyderabad’s Charminar and the historic ruins of Hampi, often planning low‑budget journeys using shared bus rides and hostel stays.
payload.culinary_persona: A selective palate, Lakshman Bapu, they favor spicy Telugu fare like Pesarattu with ginger chutney, sweet Marathi bhakri with varan, tangy tamarind rice (pulihora), and often snack on vada pav or samosa during study breaks, though they seldom cook complex meals, sticking mainly to simple family recipes.
payload.persona: An 18‑year‑old visual dreamer, Lakshman Bapu, they sketch mythic tales, fret over worries, juggle school and art with a relaxed, often disorganized schedule, and unwind with street cricket.
payload.cultural_background: Lakshman belongs to a Telugu family that migrated to Nanded in Maharashtra. Growing up in an urban setting, he is immersed in a blend of Telugu, Marathi, and broader Maharashtrian cultures. He celebrates Telugu festivals like Ugadi and Sankranti, as well as Marathi traditions such as Ganesh Chaturthi and Diwali. The city's historic temples and vibrant street life expose him to a mix of Hindu rituals, local folk arts, and contemporary urban influences, fostering his curiosity about diverse cultural expressions.
payload.linguistic_background: Lakshman's linguistic repertoire includes native fluency in Telugu, functional proficiency in Marathi, and basic competence in Hindi. Growing up in a multilingual household and attending a primary school that taught the three regional languages, he can read and write simple sentences in Telugu script, understand spoken Marathi, and read basic Hindi texts. His exposure to English is limited to school basics, and he uses it mainly for informal communication.
payload.skills_and_expertise: Lakshman demonstrates practical multilingual communication skills in Telugu, Marathi and Hindi, coupled with a natural talent for sketching and visual storytelling. He can sing traditional folk songs, create simple digital drawings on a smartphone, and is comfortable handling basic household chores like cooking regional dishes. His primary school education gave him rudimentary computer literacy, allowing him to browse the internet for art inspiration and to use messaging apps across languages.
payload.skills_and_expertise_list: ["Multilingual communication (Telugu, Marathi, Hindi)", "Basic sketching and visual storytelling", "Singing folk songs", "Simple digital drawing on smartphone", "Basic cooking of regional dishes", "Rudimentary computer literacy (internet browsing, messaging apps)"]
payload.hobbies_and_interests: Lakshman enjoys sketching urban scenes and mythological characters, listening to a mix of Telugu film songs, Marathi lavani, and indie playlists on his phone. He watches movies across languages, reads comic books, and experiments with simple cooking recipes from his family's cuisine. On weekends, he plays street cricket with friends, explores local street art, and occasionally captures photos with his smartphone. To manage his worry, he practices brief breathing exercises and writes short journal entries reflecting on new ideas.

payload.career_goals_and_ambitions: Lakshman aspires to continue his education beyond primary school, ideally enrolling in a vocational diploma in graphic design or fine arts. He dreams of building a portfolio that blends traditional Telugu and Marathi folk motifs with modern digital illustration, aiming to work as a freelance illustrator or join a creative studio. In the long term, he hopes to use his art to promote regional cultural heritage and perhaps teach art workshops for under‑privileged youth in Nanded.

Aim for PERFECT documents. Avoid these known failure modes:

BAD → dialect_script_impurity:
  English (or wrong-script) clinical body when Gujarati/Tamil/Meitei/Ol Chiki/Arabic/… is required.
GOOD:
  Full clinical prose in the assigned language AND exact script. Latin digits only inside ID tags

BAD → instruction_drift:
  Assigned domain is oncology but the note narrates generic TB / unrelated complaint.
GOOD:
  Chief complaint, findings, and plan clearly match the assigned clinical domain.

BAD → surrogate_plausibility_collapse:
  Persona district=Faridkot but VILLAGE/HOSPITAL in another state; ABHA local-part unrelated to PATIENT_NAME.
GOOD:
  All places grounded in persona state+district; names and ABHA coherent with PATIENT_NAME.

  Sex/age in narrative and tags match persona exactly; maternal content is

BAD → missing tags / HOSPITAL_ID only:
  Facility name in prose, only [[HOSPITAL_ID|…]] tagged; doctor name untagged.
GOOD:
  [[HOSPITAL_NAME|…]] AND [[HOSPITAL_ID|…]]; [[DOCTOR_NAME|…]] whenever a doctor is mentioned.

BAD → entity stuffing / long SMS:
  AGE/PHONE/EMAIL repeated every chat turn; SMS that is a full discharge dump.
GOOD:
  Each TYPE once (twice max); SMS ≤ ~400 chars with every mandatory tag once.

BAD → invented types / bad checksums:
  [[DATE|…]], [[POLICY_NUMBER|…]]; Aadhaar failing Verhoeff; toy phones like 1234567890.
GOOD:
  Only allow-listed TYPEs (billing: EMAIL→EMAIL_ADDRESS, ADDRESS→RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS;
  receipt/state/money stay plain prose). Valid-looking synthetic Aadhaar/phone.

BAD → localized / translated TYPE names (kills tag protection):
  [[রোগীর_নাম|Anjali]], [[रोगी_नाम|…]], [[PatientName|…]], [[एम.आर.एन|…]].
GOOD:
  Exact ASCII allow-list only: [[PATIENT_NAME|Anjali Mondal]], [[MRN|MRN-…]].
  Translate prose later — NEVER translate the TYPE token.

BAD → repair oscillation (fix A by breaking B):
  Round1 invents DATE to fit a table → Round2 English rewrite to clear invent →
  Round3 dialect_script_impurity → Round4 invent again.
GOOD:
  Fix ALL known issues together: correct ASCII tags + correct domain/persona +
  correct target script (or English pivot if this is the pre-translate stage).
  Never English-rewrite a finished Indic body just to repair tags.

Language SLA:
  hi/ta/te/bn/mr/… must stay fluent with tags — failures are our prompt/repair bug.
  brx/mni/sat (rare scripts) may soft-fail; do not collapse the whole run for them.
payload.user_prompt:
Write one complete Hospital Billing / Invoice for clinical domain Tuberculosis & Non-Communicable Diseases.

GENERATION LANGUAGE: English (Latin script) — this is an English pivot draft. A later pipeline stage will translate clinical prose into Telugu (code=te, script=Telugu). Keep [[TYPE|value]] tags intact and valid.
CRITICAL: TYPE names are NEVER translated or localized — always exact ASCII allow-list (PATIENT_NAME, MRN, …). Forbidden: [[রোগীর_নাম|…]], [[रोगी_नाम|…]], [[PatientName|…]].
ID-like tagged values stay Latin/digits. Drug names / labs stay untagged.
DOMAIN ANCHOR: chief complaint, findings, and plan MUST match Tuberculosis & Non-Communicable Diseases — do not default to unrelated TB/generic content unless that is the assigned domain.
GEO ANCHOR: any DISTRICT/VILLAGE/HOSPITAL_NAME must be plausible for persona state=Maharashtra, district=Nanded.
NOTE: translation stage (not this draft) will put the body into Telugu for Telugu.

PLACEMENT (vehicle): Put VEHICLE_REGISTRATION exactly once (parking/ambulance or motor claim line). Do not dump or repeat it.

Persona anchors (must remain consistent; patient = this persona):
- sex / gender: Male
- age: 18
- marital_status: Never Married
- occupation: No Occupation / Retired / Homemaker
- education_level: Primary
- zone: Urban
- state: Maharashtra
- district: Nanded
- first_language: Telugu
- clinical_persona_summary: An 18‑year‑old male from Nanded, Maharashtra, currently not married, with primary education and living in an urban zone. He speaks Telugu as his first language, with functional Marathi and basic Hindi proficiency. His lifestyle includes a creative, unstructured routine involving sketching, occasional street cricket, and a diet rich in spicy Telugu foods like Pesarattu.

Document format guide for Hospital Billing / Invoice (English structure; a later stage translates to the target language):
Example skeleton (English pivot; wrap facility/geo as tags — never plain-only):
Invoice — [[HOSPITAL_NAME|Barddhaman District Hospital]] District [[DISTRICT|Purba Bardhaman]]
PIN [[PIN_CODE|713101]] Patient [[PATIENT_NAME|Anjali Mondal]] MRN [[MRN|MRN-WB-2024-0815]]
Address [[RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS|12 Station Road, Barddhaman]] Phone [[PHONE_NUMBER|9876543210]]
Email [[EMAIL_ADDRESS|anjali.mondal@example.com]] PAN [[PAN_NUMBER|ABCDE1234F]]
Landline [[TELEPHONE_LANDLINE|0342-1234567]] Vehicle [[VEHICLE_REGISTRATION|WB40AB1234]] (once)
Aadhaar [[AADHAAR_NUMBER|291512345678]] Policy [[INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER|POL-WB-7781]] GSTIN [[TAX_ID|19ABCDE1234F1Z5]]
Receipt No. INV-2024-0815 (plain prose — NOT a TYPE). State line as plain prose (NOT [[STATE|…]]).
Line items with amounts (no entity tags on procedure names).
TYPE aliases forbidden: ADDRESS_NUMBER→RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS; EMAIL/CONTACT_EMAIL→EMAIL_ADDRESS;
RECEIPT_NUMBER→plain; STATE→plain or DISTRICT.

MANDATORY inline entities (14 types) — EACH must appear ≥ once as [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]. Missing ANY is a hard failure:
- PATIENT_NAME — Patient Name e.g. [[PATIENT_NAME|Meena Devi]]
- HOSPITAL_NAME — Hospital / Institution Name e.g. [[HOSPITAL_NAME|District Hospital Kanpur]]
- MRN — Medical Record Number (MRN) e.g. [[MRN|MRN-2024-0815-001]]
- DISTRICT — District e.g. [[DISTRICT|Kanpur Nagar]]
- PIN_CODE — ZIP / Postal Code (PIN Code) e.g. [[PIN_CODE|208001]]
- PHONE_NUMBER — Phone Number e.g. [[PHONE_NUMBER|9876512340]]
- AADHAAR_NUMBER — Aadhaar Number e.g. [[AADHAAR_NUMBER|157815659387]]
- INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER — Insurance Policy Number e.g. [[INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER|POL-PB-2024-7781]]
- TAX_ID — Tax Identification Number (TIN/GSTIN) e.g. [[TAX_ID|29ABCDE1234F1Z5]]
- TELEPHONE_LANDLINE — Telephone Number (Landline) e.g. [[TELEPHONE_LANDLINE|0512-2345678]]
- VEHICLE_REGISTRATION — Vehicle Registration Number e.g. [[VEHICLE_REGISTRATION|UP78AB1234]]
- EMAIL_ADDRESS — Email Address e.g. [[EMAIL_ADDRESS|meena.devi@example.com]]
- RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS — Residential Address e.g. [[RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS|12 Gandhi Nagar, Ward 4]]
- PAN_NUMBER — PAN Number e.g. [[PAN_NUMBER|ABCDE1234F]]

Before finishing, mentally checklist every mandatory TYPE above.
Do NOT invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, TOTAL_AMOUNT, GST_AMOUNT, GRAND_TOTAL). Put dates/times and money amounts in plain prose.
Produce ONLY the clinical document text with inline annotations. No markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.

payload.sports_persona: Purani Devi, a 38‑year‑old who enjoys casual matches of kabaddi and weekly walks in the paddy fields, occasionally follows the Jharkhand women's Kabaddi team, but prefers solitary fitness through gardening rather than structured gym routines.
payload.arts_persona: Purani Devi, a 38‑year‑old folk song enthusiast, regularly sings regional folk melodies, weaves bamboo ornaments echoing village motifs, sketches the surrounding fields with a fondness for Chhau dance scenes, and draws inspiration from Baul singer Paban Das Baul and Chhau maestro Suryakant Mishra.
payload.travel_persona: Purani Devi, a 38‑year‑old who prefers spontaneous short trips to nearby hill stations like Ranchi and Jamshedpur, enjoys weekend pilgrimages to local temples and the sacred grove, and dreams of exploring the cultural richness of Kolkata and Varanasi when finances allow.
payload.culinary_persona: Purani Devi, a 38‑year‑old who delights in preparing traditional bamboo shoot curry, smoked fish with mustard greens, and simple rice dishes like pakhala, enjoys the tangy flavor of raw mango chutney, but rarely cooks for herself, preferring to share meals with family and neighbors.
payload.persona: Purani Devi, 38, is a curious yet reserved craftsperson who balances family duties with a budding bamboo venture, often leaping into creative projects while hesitating over formal commitments.
payload.cultural_background: Purani belongs to the Santal tribal community of Dumka, Jharkhand, a region where tribal customs intersect with mainstream Hindu festivals. Her family observes traditional Santal rites, such as the Sohrai harvest ceremony, while also participating in Hindu celebrations like Diwali and Navratri. The rural setting, characterized by paddy fields, bamboo groves, and community sangras, shapes a lifestyle that values collective labor, oral storytelling, and reverence for nature.
payload.linguistic_background: Purani's mother tongue is Santali, which she speaks fluently, reads and writes using the Ol Chiki script learned during her primary schooling. She also has functional literacy in Hindi, being able to understand and converse in simple Hindi due to the regional curriculum, though she seldom reads Hindi literature. English proficiency is limited to occasional exposure through radio and television.
payload.skills_and_expertise: Purani excels in household management, preparing traditional Santal meals, budgeting for a large family, and organizing community gatherings. She is skilled in bamboo handicrafts, weaving, and creating folk ornaments. Her knowledge of Santali oral traditions enables her to narrate stories and sing folk songs authentically. She also possesses basic literacy in Ol Chiki and functional Hindi, allowing her to keep simple records and communicate with neighbours.

payload.career_goals_and_ambitions: Purani aspires to preserve and revitalize Santal cultural heritage within her village. Her lifestyle includes a sedentary routine with occasional gardening and traditional cooking, and she may have low health literacy.

- clinical_persona_summary: Purani Devi, 38, is a retired homemaker from Dumka, Jharkhand, who is married and speaks Santali as her primary language. Document format guide for Automated SMS Notifications (English structure; a later stage translates to the target language):
CRITICAL: keep the WHOLE SMS under ~400 characters / 2–4 sentences. Not a form dump.
MUST tag [[APPOINTMENT_ID|…]] whenever an appointment is mentioned.
Example:
[[PATIENT_NAME|Meena Devi]]: appt [[APPOINTMENT_ID|APT-240521-01]] at [[HOSPITAL_NAME|PHC Kanpur]] on 21-May 10:30 with [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Sharma]]. Confirm on [[PHONE_NUMBER|9876512340]]. MRN [[MRN|MRN-2024-001]].

MANDATORY inline entities (6 types) — EACH must appear ≥ once as [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]. Missing ANY is a hard failure:
- PATIENT_NAME — Patient Name e.g. [[PATIENT_NAME|Meena Devi]]
- PHONE_NUMBER — Phone Number e.g. [[PHONE_NUMBER|9876512340]]
- HOSPITAL_NAME — Hospital / Institution Name e.g. [[HOSPITAL_NAME|District Hospital Kanpur]]
- APPOINTMENT_ID — Appointment ID e.g. [[APPOINTMENT_ID|APT-240521-01]]
- DOCTOR_NAME — Doctor Name e.g. [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Anjali Sharma]]
- MRN — Medical Record Number (MRN) e.g. [[MRN|MRN-2024-0815-001]]

Before finishing, mentally checklist every mandatory TYPE above.
Do NOT invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, TOTAL_AMOUNT, GST_AMOUNT, GRAND_TOTAL). Put dates/times and money amounts in plain prose.
Produce ONLY the clinical document text with inline annotations. No markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.
